lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Oi, hold it right there. Road toll's been raised to ten gold. Goblins broke the bridge further north, y'see...

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: (He's clearly lying — the bridge is visible behind him, intact.)

pay.text: He takes your coin with a sneer and steps aside.

fight.text: He raises his blade. "Should've paid up!"

sneak_past.text: You melt into the shadows and pass by unseen. He never knew you were there.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Thornwood has changed. The wolves don't flee from torchlight anymore. Something corrupted them — the same wrongness that's taken the goblins.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: If you enter, watch the tree lines. And whatever you hear at night... don't follow it.

choices.text: Turn back; this is too dangerous.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Courage — real or foolish, I can never tell. Take this warning instead of a blade: the corruption feeds on doubt.

enter.text: He steps aside. The woods swallow the path behind you.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You'd lend an old warden your ear? Then I'll share what I know.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: There's a rift — deep in Thornwood. Something tore a hole between here and somewhere else. That's where it's pouring in.

learned.text: He presses a warden's charm into your palm.

reward.itemDesc: A gift from the Forest Warden — quietly warm to the touch.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Wise, perhaps. Or just tired. Either way — the road will still be here when you find your nerve.

leave.text: You turn back, the forest watching you go.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I have watched the Emberveil spread for thirty years. What you see is merely the symptom. The wound is somewhere else. Somewhere darker.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The goblins did not choose to be what they are now. Something reached into their minds and gave them purpose. A will from beyond.

choices.text: Accept her reading of the future.
Demand proof — her words feel practiced.
Offer 20 gold for a deeper vision.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Then go with open eyes. Carry this — a lens for the Veil.

take.text: She presses a cold glass into your hand.

reward.itemDesc: A sliver of smoked glass. The Veil cannot hide from it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Proof? Proof is a story the blind tell each other. Very well — I'll keep my gifts.

leave.text: She turns away. You feel you've lost something you cannot name.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Gold opens some doors even to the wise.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I see a rift in the south — ancient, patient. And I see three hands on a single blade at the very end.

thank.text: She smiles thinly. The coins vanish into her sleeve.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Beyond this gate lie the Dust Roads. The air itself burns here — the Emberveil's corruption runs deep in this land.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Steel your nerves. The creatures here are not what they once were.

choices.text: Stand with the sentinel in prayer.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Few travelers still remember the old rites. The ancestors will watch you down the Dust Roads.

enter.text: He steps aside. A faint warmth lingers at your back.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I... see nothing. I hear nothing. Go quickly, before I remember my orders.

paid.text: The coins disappear into his glove. He will not meet your eye.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Rude. Unwise. But your funeral, stranger.

rude.text: You cross the gate with his glare burning into your back.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Molten Plateau is no place for the living. Lava flows shift without warning, and the golems... they guard something ancient.

enter.text: He shakes his head slowly but does not stop you.

ask.text: A rift — older than the kingdom itself. It pulses with heat that no natural flame could produce.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The rift... is just ahead. I tried to seal it, but the power was too great. You must succeed where I failed.

enter.text: He slumps against the wall. "Go... quickly..."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Breach widens with every passing hour. Demons pour through — endless, relentless. This is the front line.

enter.text: She grips her blade tighter. "Then fight well."

ask.text: Find the Void Altar deep within. Destroy it, and the breach should collapse.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You stand before the Void Altar. Reality warps around it — colors bleed, sound distorts. Power radiates from its core.

destroy.text: You raise your weapon. The altar screams as the first blow lands.

study.text: Ancient runes cover its surface. This altar is a conduit — it channels power from somewhere far deeper.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You have reached the Core. Beyond this point, the veil between worlds is thinnest. The Sovereign waits within.

enter.text: The spirit fades. The path ahead glows with terrible light.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The seal pulses weakly. Whatever power held this barrier is nearly spent. Beyond it, you sense an immense presence.

break.text: The seal shatters with a sound like thunder. The way to the Sovereign is open.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Reality itself unravels here. The stars are wrong. The ground shifts beneath your feet. This is the edge of everything.

choices.text: We've come too far to turn back.

enter.text: The echo fades into silence. Only the void remains.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You dare enter my sanctum? I have seen the end of all things. I have walked between stars. You are nothing.

fight.text: He laughs — a sound like breaking glass. "Then come."

doubt.text: His eyes widen. "You... you see it too. The flaw in the pattern." His power wavers momentarily.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Gates of the Void stand open. Beyond them lies the true darkness — the source of everything that has plagued your world.

enter.text: The gates swing wide. Cold, absolute darkness swallows you whole.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Beyond lies the Abyss — where reality was born and where it ends. The Architect waits in the Primordial Nexus.

enter.text: The shade dissolves, granting passage to the depths below.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You stand at the threshold of all creation. The Architect shaped this reality — and now seeks to unmake it.

enter.text: The echo fades, and the gates of creation swing open before you.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Beyond this gate lies the Dragon's Reach — an ancient dragon kingdom carved into living mountain. Dragons rule the sky here, and their dragon-flame can melt steel from leagues away.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Dragon King, Bahamorth, has awakened. If you seek to slay a dragon, slay ALL the dragons, or die to dragon-fire — this is your road.

choices.text: We hunt dragons. Let us pass.
[INT 14] What do you know of the Dragon King?

enter.text: She steps aside. A dragon roars somewhere far above.

learn.text: Bahamorth is older than kingdoms. His dragon-hoard contains weapons forged of dragonsteel — dragon-fang blades, dragon-scale armor. You'll need every dragon-killing trick you know.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The altar glows with dragon-blood. Dragon bones hang from every pillar. The cult of the Dragon King has been waiting for you.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: "How long has this altar been bleeding?"

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: "Since before your grandmother's grandmother walked. The Dragon King's heart beats in time with it. Quiet it, and you quiet him."

choices.text: [INT 14] Read the altar-script for a weakness.

destroy.text: The altar cracks, and a dragon-howl echoes from deep within the mountain.

loot.text: The dragon-tooth amulet is yours. Power radiates from it like heat from a dragon.

reward.itemDesc: Carved from an ancient dragon fang. Hums with dragon-fire.

weakness.text: The script names the third dragon-scale over the heart as the soft one. You commit it to memory.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You reached the Dragon Throne. Bahamorth, the Dragon King, waits within his dragon-hall. No mortal has survived his dragon-fire in a thousand years.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: "And you held this door alone all that time?"

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: "I held it because no one came. You came. That is its own dragon-fire — burning the old story off the door."

choices.text: Then we will be the first.
[CHA 15] Ask him to charge the dragon at your side.

enter.text: The Dragon Knight bows once. "Slay the dragon. End the dragon age."

allied.text: "Aye. I will hold the throne-room door until you take the Dragon King's head. Strike true."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I have seen every dragon that has lived and every dragon yet to hatch. Bahamorth's dragon-heart is his weakness — it beats beneath three dragon-scales of pure dragon-steel.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Strike the dragon-heart, and the Dragon King will fall. Fail, and dragon-fire will consume your bones.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: "Which of the three scales gives?"

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: "The third. Always the third. The first two are lies. Remember that when the fire is on you."

choices.text: [INT 16] Ask for the exact rhythm of his heartbeat.
[WIS 15] Ask what the dragon-king fears most.

enter.text: She sinks back into dragon-shaped silence.

rhythm.text: "One-two-three. One-two-three. He breathes between the threes. Strike on the breath."

fear.text: "His own reflection. He has not seen it in a thousand years and he does not want to."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Please. Is there a healer among you? A priest, even a novice?

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The goblins overran our post at dawn. I'm the last.

choices.text: [Healer in party] Tend to his wounds.
We have no healer. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The healing light... thank you. I thought I'd die in the mud.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Take my lieutenant's seal. If you reach Emberglen, show it to the garrison commander.

healed.text: He clasps your hand once, fiercely. Then he sleeps.

reward.itemDesc: A cracked brass disc — still warm.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I understand. Then sit with me, just for a moment.

stayed.text: He does not die alone. A small mercy.

left.text: You walk away. You don't look back.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: His weight is considerable, but you manage.

carried.text: You set him under a tree where a passing cart will find him. It's the best you can do.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Word travels in these woods. They say you entered Thornwood without flinching.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Courage is not common. I'd like to ask something of you.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: My apprentice went north three days ago and has not returned. If you find any sign of him — bring it back.

agree.text: He presses a warden's token into your hand. "Show this to the northern posts — they'll let you through."

reward.itemDesc: Opens doors in the northern forest.

price.text: He names two hundred gold. You argue him to one-fifty. A deal.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Fair enough. You owe this wood nothing.

leave.text: He steps aside. The path is clear.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Enchantments! Very legal enchantments! The realest kind! Only fifty gold, barely cursed at all!

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Excellent choice! You'll feel the power any moment now. Any. Moment.

wait.text: Nothing happens. Then something small crackles in your chest. You feel oddly alert.

refund.text: He gives back forty gold with enormous reluctance.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Oh, you see through me. Good. Real price: twenty gold. Actual benefit: minor and temporary.

smart_buy.text: You hand him twenty gold and feel a genuine flicker of something in your blood.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You're making a mistake! A very safe mistake!

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Stones stacked by careful hands. The warden's charm in your pack hums faintly in response.

choices.text: Press the charm against the cairn.

open.text: The stones shift. Inside: a sealed pouch of silver and a rune-scratched ring. A warden's cache, left for emergencies.

reward.itemDesc: A ring with a single carved rune. It feels old.

leave.text: The cairn sits undisturbed. The charm hums and then quiets.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You see me. So few can. The Veil is thinning here — or you are unusually hard to kill.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I am what remains of a hedge-knight who met the Veil fifty years before its time. A pact went wrong. I could use your strength.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You feel a cold settling in your chest — not painful, merely permanent. You carry a little of me now.

accepted.text: The shade fades. Your skin is slightly cooler than before.

reward.itemDesc: A ring of cold iron. You feel watched.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: A small fraction of your vitality. You will not notice. The benefit — I will warn you before certain dangers.

accepted_informed.text: Fair deal. The shade nods once and fades.

declined.text: It shrugs — or tries to, without solid shoulders. "Wise."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Understandable. I would not trust me either.

left.text: The shade wavers and dissipates. You feel the road is slightly less crowded.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I'll be damned! You made it past the herald. I've been telling everyone in Emberglen about you.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Well — I told two people. But still.

choices.text: Ask if he's rebuilt his business.

ask.text: He pulled in a favour, salvaged some stock. A crate of supplies now rides on a borrowed cart. "You have my thanks and ten percent off anything I carry."

thanks.text: He pumps your hand vigorously. You feel it in your wrist for an hour.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: A word. My man sent a runner — the one you carried off the road. He wants you to have this.

accept.text: He hands you a garrison token and a purse. "You're a friend of the post. That means something out here."

reward.itemDesc: Admits one to any garrison storehouse.

refuse.text: He insists. You compromise and take only the token. He respects this.

reward.itemDesc: Admits one to any garrison storehouse.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Please! Are any of you mages? Real mages? I've been trying to find one for weeks.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: My master died before teaching me the third form of Arcane Bolt. I've been stuck at the second form for months.

choices.text: [Arcane caster] Show him the third form.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Yes! Yes, that's it — I can feel the current. Thank you, thank you, THANK—

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: He accidentally fires the third form into a passing cart. The cart is fine. The wheel is not.

fled.text: You walk very quickly away. He shouts his thanks from behind a cloud of smoke.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Oh. Right. Of course. Sorry to bother you.

left.text: He slumps against the wall with the specific posture of someone practising disappointment.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: This is... not quite right. But it's closer than I've ever been. I can work from this.

sketched.text: He takes the sketch with trembling hands. You feel you've done a small but genuine good.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A roadside shrine — smashed. The idol inside has been replaced with a lump of scorched coal.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Something in the coal stirs. A wrongness, small but deliberate.

choices.text: Smash the coal and move on.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Light floods the alcove. The coal crumbles to ordinary ash.

purified.text: The shrine hums quietly with restored purpose. You feel it acknowledged you.

reward.itemDesc: A ring the shrine pressed to your hand while you worked.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Veil-cult script. A binding, meant to anchor a minor haunt to the road and delay travelers.

unraveled.text: You work the binding loose in five minutes. The coal cracks. The haunt dissolves.

documented.text: A sketch for the warden. Knowledge is its own weapon.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The coal shatters. Something small and angry disperses.

smashed.text: It worked. The road feels less watched.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Someone else's problem. The road is long.

left.text: You feel the wrongness at your back for the next mile.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Ah — you're the ones who stood with Darven at the gate. He told me. Old rites, still observed. That's rarer than it should be.

choices.text: We don't know what you mean.

thanks.text: He clasps both hands over yours and presses a soldier's ring into your palm. "May the road return it someday."

reward.itemDesc: Worn by those who still know the old words.

unknown.text: He studies your face. "You were there. I'm sure of it." He lets it go.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Forty of us in that barn, cold and too tired to sleep. We've been walking for six days.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: If you have anything — a song, a story, anything — I'll give what little I have left.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The barn goes quiet. Then — singing. Forty voices joining yours one by one.

sang.text: They sleep well that night. You don't need coin for that. But they give what they have anyway.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: They lean in. Children, elders, everyone. You make something up. It doesn't matter — they believe it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: He stares at the coins like he's forgotten what they're for. Then memory returns.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: He nods. He expected nothing. He is past disappointment.

left.text: You carry the weight of that for a while.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Oh. Ah. This — this is not what it looks like. Unless it looks like I'm stealing from a warlord's supply cache. In which case it is exactly what it looks like.

choices.text: Turn him in for a reward.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Your hands are steadier than mine. The lock barely whispered.

split.text: The cache yields coin, rations, and one small curiosity — a ring with a lock-pick engraved on the band.

reward.itemDesc: A ring worn by the warlord's own quartermaster.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: He looks resigned. "Fair enough. I expected this end."

turned.text: The garrison gives you thirty gold and a formal thanks. The thief gives you a look that costs more.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: "You're an interesting kind of person."

left.text: Behind you, a soft click. He got in. Well done, you suppose.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The Veil Lens in your pack hums — loud, insistent. Through the crack in the rock, you can see beyond the Dust Roads into somewhere else.

look.text: For three seconds you see the other side: a vast burning plain, something vast and patient moving across it. Then the lens cools and the vision fades. You understand now that this is not a border. It is a wound.

back.text: You feel it even with your eyes closed. The wrongness is directional.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Forty-odd graves, unmarked. The Veil-corruption in the soil is thin but present.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Left unaddressed, the bodies will turn by the next new moon.

choices.text: [Dark caster] Seal the graves against turning.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The air around the graves settles — resistant, then resigned. The corruption is bound.

sealed.text: The graves go quiet. Whatever was stirring rests again. This is what necromancers are actually for.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: You drive a notched stick into the earth at the head of the field. Someone else may deal with this.

marked.text: It is insufficient. But you did something.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: You look at the graves for a moment that feels longer than it is.

left.text: The graves stay quiet — for now.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Mira's vision returns without warning. The rift in the south. Three hands on a blade. Then: a name, scratched into stone just ahead.

search.text: You find it in ten minutes. Scratched into a waystone, half-buried: "Ember — not a place. A plan." Below that, a sealed iron capsule with a ring inside.

reward.itemDesc: A ring with a single word etched inside: Patience.

shake.text: The vision scatters. You keep moving. You remember the rift in the south.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Ah — travelers. I have a proposition. My wagon is buried axle-deep in the ash. Thirty gold says you'll either help or have no cash.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The wagon groans, then surrenders. The forge-master applauds with the enthusiasm of a man who did no work.

choices.text: Collect the deposit refund and bonus.

heaved.text: "As promised — plus a little extra for the quality of the heave." He hands you a forged ring, still warm from the wagon furnace.

reward.itemDesc: Made by an ashen forge-master in the back of a stuck wagon.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Ten gold. A fair counter for an unfair situation.

choices.text: Push at ten gold — no muscle needed.

pushed.text: He finds three travelers willing to do it for fifteen. You save fifteen gold and find this outcome morally neutral.

dec.text: He shrugs and returns to waiting.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: "Next time, then!"

walked.text: His voice fades behind a drift of ash.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The cold in your chest stirs. The shade you bound in Act 1 speaks — just for a moment, just enough.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: "The Archfiend reads fire. Lead with frost. He hates things that can't be burned."

heard.text: The cold quiets. The warning costs the shade nothing. It was the minimum it could do.

wrong.text: A draft in the tunnel. Nothing more.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: A Demon Hunter. I can smell the burning purpose on you.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Kill me and I scream. Every imp in a mile radius will hear. Spare me and I tell you where the Archfiend keeps his heart.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: His left gauntlet. A scar in the shape of a lock. Strike it — and he cannot heal. You're welcome.

deal_done.text: It scurries off. You will probably regret this. Or you won't.

betrayed.text: It screams. Very loudly. Several imps arrive. This is now a problem of your own making.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: It was right about the screaming.

killed.text: You win the skirmish. The information dies with it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: That's fine. I'll find someone else to betray my master to.

left.text: Its laughter echoes in the tunnel.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The thing you saw through Mira's lens — the vast patient shape — you see its shadow again, briefly, overlaid on the hellfire of the breach.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: It is further away than it should be. It is closer than it was. It noticed you noticing it.

record.text: You write in the margin of whatever you're carrying: "Patient. Directional. Aware." Then you close the book and keep walking.

push.text: The shadow retreats. You don't think you pushed it. You think it stepped back.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I acquire things. Things from behind doors that shouldn't be opened, from ruins that shouldn't be entered.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I have one item today. A ring, recovered from the Pit Tyrant's own treasury. Asking price: a hundred gold.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: No questions. No receipt. You understand.

bought.text: The ring is cold and heavy. It feels like something that should not leave the place it came from.

reward.itemDesc: The coldest ring you've ever held. Does not warm.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Sixty gold. You have a talent for this that borders on aggressive.

haggled.text: You get the ring for sixty. It is still very cold.

reward.itemDesc: The coldest ring you've ever held. Does not warm.

walked.text: He doesn't call after you. He knows you'll think about it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Someone else will want it. They always do.

left.text: You wonder about the ring for the rest of the day.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: A hellhound took two of my men into the ruins. I've got their tracks, but no woodsman to follow them.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You have a ranger, from the looks of you. I'll pay. Just tell me if there's anything left to find.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The tracks go straight to a collapsed cellar. The hound is gone — moved on. The two soldiers are dead but intact.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: You find a locked box in the cellar beside them. They must have stumbled on it.

reported.text: He thanks you quietly. The box belongs to the garrison now. He gives you fifty gold and means it.

kept.text: You tell him the soldiers are dead and the hound is gone. You say nothing about the box. It opens later: coin and a sealed letter you probably shouldn't have.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Fair. I wouldn't send my own family.

left.text: He returns to staring at the ruins.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I've been practising since you showed me the third form. I've... been practising a lot. The fourth form came to me in a dream.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Would you like to see it? Most of the fire has gone out now.

watch.text: The fourth form is genuinely impressive and only marginally catastrophic. He gives you a ring from the rubble of what used to be a wall.

reward.itemDesc: Recovered from a minor magical incident. Barely singed.

decline.text: "Probably wise." He looks disappointed but safe.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A forge burning with Malgrath's own fire — pure demonic heat, older than the breach itself. Every smith's nightmare and every warrior's dream.

choices.text: [CON 16] Plunge your weapon into the forge.

forge.text: The metal shrieks. When you pull it free it glows red and will not cool. The next fight it fights harder than you do.

leave.text: The forge stares back. You stare back harder. A draw.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Word travels. You have friends at the post. That means you have friends here.

accept.text: They resupply you, sharpen your weapons, and press a sealed letter into your hand — addressed to the garrison at the Breach. Insurance, the commander calls it.

reward.itemDesc: Sealed with wax. Insurance, you were told.

unknown.text: He shrugs. "Shame. Let us know when you change your mind."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You looked through the lens. You recorded what you saw. Few do both.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I have no name you could pronounce. I am patient. I am directional. And I am — provisionally — not your enemy.

ask.text: It is silent for a long moment. "Nothing yet. When I do, I will say so clearly." Then it is gone and the void feels ordinary again.

reject.text: "Noted." It leaves. You feel it hasn't gone far.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Warlock. I know the shape of your agreements. I have one to propose.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Power, now. A favour, later. Unspecified. That is the honest version.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Done. The power is yours. The favour is mine to name. I am not in a hurry.

sealed.text: Something shifts behind your eyes. The void feels closer and less hostile. This is possibly not better.

reward.itemDesc: A ring of compressed nothingness. It is heavy.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: "I will remember this. Not with malice. With patience."

rejected.text: The entity dissolves. You have the sense of an appointment cancelled rather than an enemy made.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Silence. Then: "Interesting. No. But — interesting."

released.text: You had it, briefly. It let you release it. A gift disguised as a failure.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Watcher leaves a memory in your mind — not words exactly, more like a shape of knowledge.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Unraveler was not always the Unraveler. There was a name, before. The name was taken from it as a punishment.

hold.text: You will not forget. The Unraveler is a prisoner as much as a threat. This changes nothing tactical. It changes something else.

dismiss.text: The memory fades. You feel it was important.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A tear in time — not space. Through it you can see this same corridor, six hours ago, empty.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Someone with sufficient temporal training could walk through it safely. Or unsafely. The distinction is training-dependent.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Six hours ago, this corridor was a library. A Veil-cult library, abandoned in haste. Books everywhere. You take three that look useful and step back.

returned.text: The rift seals. The books exist in the present now.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The rift closes on its own after a minute.

passed.text: You have a headache that takes until camp to fade.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The favour. I am ready to name it. You will carry a message for me, written in a language you cannot read, to someone you will meet in Act 5. You will not open it.

choices.text: Open the message now, on principle.

agree.text: The message appears in your pack, sealed with a substance that is not wax.

open.text: Inside: a circle with a single dot inside it. You understand nothing. The entity says: "Good. Now deliver it anyway."

refuse.text: "I accept your refusal. I withdraw the earlier power."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The stars speak to those with sense to listen. I have listened too long. I cannot unknow what I know.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I will tell you the shape of the rift. Not to help you — I cannot anymore. But so someone living knows it.

choices.text: You're not equipped to hear this.

listen.text: He speaks for three minutes in a language that shouldn't be possible. You understand every word. You immediately forget the words but retain the shape of what they meant. The Emberveil is a door, not a wound. Someone built it.

leave.text: He nods. "Wiser than I was." He goes back to watching stars that have moved since he last checked.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You've seen through the lens. You know where the rift origin began. You are one of perhaps four people alive with both pieces.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I study the Veil academically. I would very much like to compare notes.

choices.text: What do you offer in return?

share.text: Two hours later, you understand twice as much and cannot explain why either of you feels better. You do anyway.

barter.text: She offers seventy gold and a detailed diagram. You accept. The diagram becomes one of the most useful things you own.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I was days from turning — the Veil had almost taken me. Then the corruption in the graves was bound. I woke up still myself.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I have been looking for you since.

thanks.text: She gives you a ring she would have been buried in. "I don't need it now."

reward.itemDesc: A ring worn by someone who almost didn't come back.

deny.text: She knows it was. She leaves the ring on the wall anyway.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I have been waiting for a delivery. Sealed with something that isn't wax. Is that from you or have you merely carried it?

deliver.text: She reads it in two seconds. "Good. This was necessary." She gives you something in return — she calls it "reciprocal consideration."

reward.itemDesc: A ring of reciprocal consideration. Its terms are unclear.

ask.text: "A circle with a dot. The punctuation of a very old language. It means: I see you. Deliver it — or don't. The message has already been read."

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: You stand before the Unraveler. You know something it did not expect: the shape of what its name once was.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: This is not power over it. But it is the kind of knowledge that makes an entity of this age pause.

choices.text: Speak the shape of its name.

speak.text: The Unraveler stills for exactly three seconds. In those three seconds it does not attack. Your first round is free.

fight.text: Straightforward. Honest. It almost seems to respect it.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: An inscription on the abyss wall — very old, in a language that has not been spoken since before the Veil.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: You understand it. You shouldn't. The star-prophet must have left more behind than you knew.

read.text: The door was built by the Architect — not this Architect, an earlier one. The lock was broken deliberately. The builder regretted it. The inscription is an apology, not a boast.

leave.text: The inscription watches you go. It has been waiting to be read for a very long time.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The pool shows only strangers. Unless you have someone capable of reading whose memories these are.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Oracle kneels. Places one hand in the pool. The water goes absolutely still.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: "These are the memories of the dead Architect. Not the one below — the first one. The builder. These are the things they could not carry into death."

saw.text: The Oracle is silent for a long time. "They were sorry. Very sorry. For all of it. That's all that's left."

stop.text: "Good." The oracle withdraws their hand. "Some memories should stay where they are."

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The pool reflects your face and keeps its secrets.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I heard about your notes. The combined data — yours and the analyst from Act 4 — points at a specific resonance in the Architect's defenses.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I've built something from that data. It is not elegant. But it may work.

accept.text: She hands you a ring set with a chip of broken resonator. It hums at a frequency your teeth notice.

reward.itemDesc: Made from converging data. Annoying frequency. Effective.

cost.text: Nothing. She wants the Architect to die. "That's sufficient payment."

reward.itemDesc: Made from converging data. Annoying frequency. Effective.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The weapon forged in Malgrath's hellfire vibrates when you cross into the Primordial Nexus. It recognises something here.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The primordial fire and the hellfire have the same source. Older than both Malgrath and the Nexus.

lead.text: It leads you to a sealed door three minutes off the main path. Inside: a cache left by a pre-Architect explorer who felt the same pull and followed it.

ignore.text: The weapon subsides. You reach your destination slightly faster.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You know about the builder. The first Architect.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I did not know that was still... in the pool. I assumed it had decayed.

choices.text: Tell it what the oracle saw.

tell.text: "Sorry." It considers this. "That changes nothing. But it is... better than not knowing." The fight begins. But it begins differently.

fight.text: The Architect fights with the clean efficiency of something that has never been surprised.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The message I received — and the inscription you read — are from the same hand. The builder left breadcrumbs.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The breadcrumbs lead to one conclusion: the Architect below is not the enemy. The door is the enemy. The Architect is the lock.

process.text: You understand something most people who fight the Architect never do: winning is correct. The how is the hard part. You go in knowing this.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Stand still, traveler — I've watched you crest the rise three times today. The Veil bends around you. Are you the one Mira spoke of?

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Mira? The seer in Emberglen?

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: She named you in her last reading before her tongue went silver. The Border Roads east of here are crawling with Veilspawn — twisted goblins, then a herald that wears them like a mantle.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: And you waited here to warn me?

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I waited to give you something. A debt I owe to the road. You'll need it before sundown — pick what suits your hand.

choices.text: A purse of coin to barter my way through.
A magic-touched ring or amulet from your pack.
A weapon befitting the way I fight.

gift_gold.text: Silas presses a heavy coin purse into your palm. "Buy your way out of the first mistake. The second one is on you."

gift_jewelry.text: He unwraps a bone-white ring etched with a sigil that hums when you slip it on. "Worn by a scout who didn't come back. Wear it better than she did."

gift_weapon.text: He sets a bundle at your feet — steel and oak in shapes you know. "Picked from the chapel reliquary. The Watchers won't miss it." You shoulder the gift.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Stand still, traveler — I've watched you crest the rise three times today. The Veil bends around you. Are you the one Mira spoke of?

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Mira? The seer in Emberglen?

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: She named you in her last reading before her tongue went silver. The Border Roads east of here are crawling with Veilspawn — twisted goblins, then a herald that wears them like a mantle.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: And you waited here to warn me?

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I waited to give you something. A debt I owe to the road. You'll need it before sundown — pick what suits your hand.

choices.text: A purse of coin to barter my way through.
A magic-touched ring or amulet from your pack.
A weapon befitting the way I fight.

gift_gold.text: Silas presses a heavy coin purse into your palm. "Buy your way out of the first mistake. The second one is on you."

gift_jewelry.text: He unwraps a bone-white ring etched with a sigil that hums when you slip it on. "Worn by a scout who didn't come back. Wear it better than she did."

gift_weapon.text: He sets a bundle at your feet — steel and oak in shapes you know. "Picked from the chapel reliquary. The Watchers won't miss it." You shoulder the gift.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Cogs and gears, friend! I fix what's broken and sell what ain't — yet.

buy.text: He hands you a bundle of salvaged parts. Crude, but usable.

leave.text: He shrugs and rattles on down the road.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Healing draughts, traveler. Brewed fresh this morning from moonpetal and fen-root.

buy.text: She hands over a warm vial. It smells of honey and earth.

discount.text: She grins. "You know your herbs! Half price for a fellow scholar." You get two vials for the price of one.

buy_normal.text: She squints at you. "Just buy or don't." You take one potion.

leave.text: She returns to her mortar and pestle without a word.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Steel. Good steel. Not that pig-iron the militia hands out.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Fifty gold gets you something that'll actually cut.

buy.text: He slaps a short blade into your hand. It's heavier than it looks. Good.

discount.text: He laughs as you pin his wrist. "Fair's fair. Take a blade, half price."

insult.text: "Weak grip for a warrior." He charges full price. You pay begrudgingly.

leave.text: He spits and resharpens a dagger.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I trade in things people don't want others to know they need.

browse.text: He produces a vial of shimmering black liquid. "Voidtouch Elixir. Doubles your next strike — but it burns going down."

detect.text: His eyes flicker — literally. Something inhuman peers through them. "Perceptive. Here — a gift, so you forget what you saw."

leave.text: He vanishes the moment you turn away. When you look back, only footprints remain.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: No stab! Skrit is trader, not fighter! Skrit has shinies!

trade.text: Skrit hands you a handful of mismatched but surprisingly useful trinkets.

threaten.text: Skrit shrieks and bolts into the underbrush, dropping a few coins in his panic.

leave.text: Skrit waves a tiny hand and disappears into a hollow log.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I seek fragments of the old world. Before the Veil. Before the fire.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: If you find anything... unusual... bring it to me. I pay well.

choices.text: Sell him information about the Veil.

ask.text: He shows you a shard of glass that shows a different sky when you look through it. "The world before," he whispers.

sell_info.text: He listens intently, scribbling notes. "Invaluable. Here — you've earned this."

leave.text: He returns to cataloging bone fragments with unsettling tenderness.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Hot stew! Best you'll find between here and the grave. Five gold a bowl.

eat.text: It's genuinely excellent. Warmth spreads through your bones.

ask.text: "Don't ask questions you don't want answered." She ladles you a free sample. It's delicious.

leave.text: She shrugs. "More for me."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I move goods that don't like being moved. Discretion guaranteed.

buy.text: He slips a lockpick set into your hand. "You didn't get these from me."

steal.text: Your fingers close around his coin pouch before he blinks. Easy.

caught.text: He catches your wrist. "Try that again and you lose the hand." He shoves you away.

leave.text: He melts back into the shadows.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: We were bound for Emberglen when the road... shifted. Nothing looks right anymore.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Please — take what you need. We just want to get home alive.

choices.text: [INT 12] The Veil is distorting this area.

help.text: You guide them back to a familiar landmark. They thank you with supplies.

take.text: They watch with hollow eyes as you take what you need. The road claims many.

explain.text: Understanding the distortion, you lead them safely and earn their deep gratitude.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Ember crystals. Harvested from the cooling flows. They hold fire like a memory.

buy.text: The crystal pulses with warmth in your hand. You feel it strengthening you.

ask.text: "The lava fields. Most harvesters don't come back. That's why the price is fair."

leave.text: She tucks the crystals back into their heat-proof case.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I carry things from the other side. Things that shouldn't exist here. But they do.

choices.text: How are you still alive in here?
Nothing from the Void is worth the risk.

browse.text: He opens a case that seems deeper than it should be. Inside: a shard of frozen starlight.

ask.text: "Alive is a generous word." His hand passes through the wall beside him. He's not entirely here.

leave.text: He nods. "Wise, perhaps. Or merely fearful."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The forest provides, if you know where to look. I know where to look.

buy.text: She wraps a bundle of healing herbs in broad leaves. "Chew, don't swallow whole."

identify.text: "Sharp eyes! That one's dreamcap — rare. Take it, you've earned it."

wrong.text: "That's nightbane, dear. You'd be dead in an hour." She sells you the right ones for full price.

leave.text: She hums a tune and disappears between the trees.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The heat here is perfect for forging. I've been working the lava vents for years.

repair.text: He hammers your gear back into shape with frightening precision.

buy_best.text: He hands you a blade that glows faintly orange. "Lava-tempered. It'll cut through anything mortal."

leave.text: He grunts and returns to his anvil.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Scrolls! Maps! Histories of places that no longer exist! Knowledge is power, friend.

buy.text: He hands you a map fragment. It shows a route you haven't seen before.

forbidden.text: His eyes widen. "You know of them? Then take this — it speaks of the first Veil breach."

normal.text: "Everything I sell is perfectly legal. Mostly." He gives you a standard map.

leave.text: He rolls up his scrolls with practiced fingers.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: War is hell. But it's also extremely profitable.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I've got salvage from the front lines. Weapons, armor, things pried from dead hands.

buy.text: The gear is dented but functional. Better than nothing.

moralize.text: "The dead don't need swords, friend. The living do." He shrugs and counts his coins.

leave.text: He's already haggling with someone else before you turn away.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: This road belongs to us now. Pay the toll or bleed.

pay.text: They step aside with mocking bows. Cowards preying on travelers.

fight.text: Steel rings on steel. They chose the wrong target today.

escape.text: You slip through their ranks before they can react. Shouts fade behind you.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Wolves. At least six of them, circling. Their eyes glow with unnatural light.

choices.text: [STR 10] Intimidate them with a battle cry.

fight.text: The alpha lunges. No more waiting.

scare.text: Your roar echoes off the rocks. The pack hesitates, then slinks away into the mist.

distract.text: They tear into the food. You back away slowly while they feast.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: An arrow thuds into the tree beside your head. Goblins in the branches, more on the ground.

choices.text: [DEX 14] Roll behind cover and flank them.

fight.text: You crash through their line. They weren't expecting aggression.

flank.text: You circle behind their archers and scatter them. The rest flee in panic.

retreat.text: You pull back, but not before a crude arrow catches your shoulder.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Webs everywhere. Thick, sticky strands between the trees. Something moves in the canopy above.

choices.text: [INT 12] Study the web pattern to find a safe path.

burn.text: Fire drives them out — screeching, furious, and very much alive.

sneak.text: A strand brushes your arm. The vibration brings them all at once.

safe_path.text: You map the dead zones in the web and pass through without touching a single strand.

trapped.text: You misjudge the pattern and walk straight into an anchor line. They descend instantly.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: We left the garrison. They can hang us for that. But we're not going back to die for nothing.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Give us your supplies and walk. Nobody gets hurt.

choices.text: [INT 14] Convince them to join you instead.

pay.text: They take it and disappear. Survival makes villains of us all.

fight.text: They draw weapons. Desperate men fight hardest.

recruit.text: "You're heading into the fire? ...Fine. At least it's a cause." They share what intelligence they have.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The ash swirls into a shape — humanoid, but wrong. Hollow eyes burn with pale fire.

choices.text: [INT 14] Attempt to banish it with willpower.

fight.text: It shrieks — a sound like tearing metal — and attacks.

banish.text: You push your will against it. It screams, fragments, and dissolves back into ash. Something glints where it stood.

flee.text: You turn and run. Cold fingers rake your back before the distance saves you.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The ground splits. Imps pour from the cracks, giggling with needle-toothed glee.

choices.text: [STR 16] Collapse the fissure with a boulder.

fight.text: They swarm you in a chittering mass.

collapse.text: You heave a massive stone over the fissure. It seals with a satisfying crunch. Muffled shrieks below.

retreat.text: You find a narrow passage. They can only come at you one at a time — but they keep coming.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Veil will claim all. You can kneel now or be consumed later.

fight.text: "So be it." Their hands crackle with dark energy.

demoralize.text: Doubt flickers in their eyes. Two acolytes lower their hands and flee. The rest are shaken.

trick.text: You drop to one knee — then drive your blade upward. The surprise gives you the advantage.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Your gold or your life. Simple transaction.

pay.text: He pockets the gold and vanishes into the brush. Efficient.

fight.text: He's faster than he looks. But not fast enough.

quick_draw.text: Your blade is at his throat before his hand reaches his hilt. He drops his weapon and runs.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The oak splits open. What steps out was a tree once — now it's a nightmare of bark and black sap.

fight.text: Roots erupt from the ground as it swings a limb like a battering ram.

burn.text: Fire catches the black sap. It screams — a sound no tree should make — but burns fast.

purify.text: You spot a pulsing black node in its trunk. One precise strike shatters it. The tree collapses, purified.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Something is following you. You can't see it, but you feel it — a presence pressing against reality like a finger testing cloth.

fight.text: It tears through the membrane of space. All teeth. All hunger.

trace.text: You find its anchor point and sever it. The stalker is flung back into the void, howling.

ignore.text: It follows for hours before losing interest. You don't sleep well that night.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The dead march in formation. Someone — something — gave them orders they still follow.

fight.text: They turn in unison. Empty sockets lock onto you. They charge.

wait.text: They march past without seeing you. One drops a coin purse.

command.text: "HALT." Your voice carries authority. They stop. Then crumble, whatever magic held them spent.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Three shapes made of living coal. Their growls sound like crackling embers.

fight.text: They leap, trailing sparks and ash.

water.text: Steam erupts as you empty your waterskin. One goes down, but the others circle.

scatter.text: You launch a burning log into their midst. They yelp and bolt, confused by the competing fire.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: It has too many limbs. Too many eyes. Looking at it makes your thoughts slide sideways.

choices.text: Attack while you can still think.

fight.text: Reality warps around you as it screams in a frequency your bones feel.

focus.text: You pierce through its psychic haze and strike at its core essence. It shrieks and folds back into the void.

madness.text: Your mind buckles. When you come to, it's gone — but so is some of your blood.

flee.text: You run until your lungs burn. The whispers follow you for miles.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Run... while I... can still... hold it...

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: His eyes flash red. Whatever was holding the demon back just lost.

choices.text: Put him down. It's a mercy.

fight.text: The demon takes full control. The knight's body moves with inhuman speed.

exorcise.text: You speak words of binding. The demon screams and tears free, dissolving. The knight collapses, alive. "Thank you..."

flee.text: Behind you, the knight screams — then silence. You don't look back.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A heavy iron chest, half-buried in rubble. The lock is rusted but intact.

open.text: The lock clicks. Inside: gold and a tarnished ring.

smash.text: The hinges shatter. Gold spills across the ground.

broken.text: Your pick snaps. The lock holds.

hurt.text: You wrench your shoulder. The chest doesn't budge.

leave.text: Some secrets are better left locked.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The rope bridge sways over a chasm. Half the planks are missing. The other half are rotten.

sprint.text: Your feet barely touch each plank. The bridge collapses behind you. Made it.

climb.text: Hand over hand, you crawl across the ropes. Your arms burn, but you make it.

fall.text: The wood gives way. You catch yourself on a rope, but the impact hurts.

detour.text: The long way around costs time but saves bones.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A door sealed with rotating stone rings. Runes glow faintly in sequence — a combination lock from another age.

solve.text: The rings align. The door grinds open, revealing a cache of ancient gold.

wrong.text: A rune flashes red. Pain lances through your hand.

break.text: Stone shatters. Brute force has its merits. Gold gleams inside.

sealed.text: The stone is harder than it looks. The door remains shut.

leave.text: The runes fade as you walk away. Another mystery for another day.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A narrow trail descends a sheer cliff face. Something glints on a ledge halfway down.

ledge.text: Your fingers find holds in the rock. On the ledge: a pouch of gems.

rappel.text: You descend smoothly and grab the cache. Climbing back up is easy.

slip.text: Your grip fails. You tumble, catching yourself painfully on a root.

leave.text: Whatever's down there can stay down there.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The corridor ahead has subtle grooves in the floor. Pressure plates. Classic.

disarm.text: Click. Click. Click. Three traps neutralized. You find salvage from previous victims.

map.text: You trace the pattern and step carefully. On the other side: untouched treasure.

triggered.text: A blade swings from the wall. You dodge most of it.

leave.text: Discretion over valor.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Road's closed. Goblin activity ahead. Turn around.

choices.text: [INT 12] Convince him you can handle it.

convince.text: "...Fine. But I warned you." He steps aside and slips you a map of goblin positions.

denied.text: "I said no. Do I need to repeat myself?"

bribe.text: He pockets the coins without expression. "I didn't see you pass."

leave.text: You turn back. There must be another way.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The river runs fast and cold. No bridge. Stepping stones break the surface, slick with spray.

hop.text: Stone to stone, light as a cat. Something gleams in the riverbed — a gold coin caught in the rocks.

wade.text: The current hammers your legs but you power through. Cold, wet, but across.

splash.text: You slip on the third stone and go under. The cold hits like a fist.

swept.text: The river drags you downstream before you catch a root. Bruised but alive.

ford.text: A safer crossing, but it costs time.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The cliff above groans. Pebbles skitter down. The whole face could go at any moment.

choices.text: [STR 16] Hold a boulder aside for the group.
[DEX 14] Wait, then pick through the rubble for ore.

sprint.text: You clear the danger zone as tons of rock crash behind you. That was close.

hold.text: You brace against the boulder. Muscles scream. The party gets through.

hit.text: A rock clips your shoulder. You stumble through, bleeding.

crushed.text: The weight is too much. You dive clear, but not cleanly.

scavenge.text: Among the settled stones: a vein of raw iron and a traveler's lost coin purse.

wait.text: Hours pass before it settles. You lose time and scrape your hands for nothing.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A shimmering barrier blocks the passage. Ancient magic — but fraying at the edges.

unravel.text: The ward dissolves thread by thread. Behind it: a sealed vault with ancient gold.

backlash.text: The ward retaliates. Lightning arcs through your body.

force.text: You charge through. The barrier shatters around you like glass. Treasure beyond.

shocked.text: The ward holds and punishes your attempt.

leave.text: Some doors are sealed for a reason.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Thick green mist rolls through the valley ahead. It smells of copper and decay.

choices.text: [INT 12] Identify the toxin and find a countermeasure.
[DEX 14] Hold your breath and sprint through.
Wait for the wind to shift.

counter.text: You crush fenroot under your nose — a natural filter. The mist parts harmlessly around you.

sprint.text: Lungs burning, you burst through the other side into clean air. Made it.

breathe.text: The mist sears your throat. You stagger through, coughing blood.

wait.text: The wind shifts after an hour. The mist thins enough to pass safely.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: These ruins are a deathtrap. Every step could bring the ceiling down. But you can see gold glinting in the dust.

navigate.text: You tread like a ghost. The ruins hold. The gold is yours.

calculate.text: You map the safe zones and extract the treasure without disturbing a single stone.

collapse.text: A pillar buckles. You dive clear but catch a face full of stone dust.

leave.text: You back away as a wall collapses behind you. Good instincts.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A stone coffin covered in warning glyphs. Whatever's inside, someone went to great lengths to keep it sealed.

read.text: "Here lies Verath, who stole from gods." Inside: his ill-gotten treasures, still gleaming.

curse.text: The glyphs flash. A cold voice whispers: "Thief." Pain wracks your body.

open.text: The lid grinds aside. Gold, jewels, and a warning you can't read.

stuck.text: The lid won't move. The glyphs seem to pulse with amusement.

leave.text: Some graves should stay closed.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Molten rock crawls across the path. Cooled islands of obsidian dot the flow — a treacherous crossing.

choices.text: [INT 14] Find a thermal updraft to predict safe paths.

leap.text: Your boots sizzle on the obsidian but you make it across. On the far side: a vein of raw ore.

predict.text: You trace the cooling patterns and find the thickest islands. A safe, if slow, crossing.

burn.text: The obsidian cracks. Liquid fire licks your legs before you scramble clear.

leave.text: The heat alone is reason enough to turn back.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I am what remains when everything is taken. I am what exists before anything is made. What am I?

correct.text: "Yes. And now you understand what you walk through." It fades, leaving a crystallized thought behind.

wrong.text: It sighs — a sound like collapsing stars. "Close enough. But understanding eludes you." Pain.

close.text: "Almost. But nothing is still something to name." It vanishes, taking your certainty with it.

fight.text: Your blade passes through it. It laughs — a sound that erases color — and disappears.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I fought in the Ember War, thirty years back. We thought it was over. We were fools.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Veil was thin then too. But it healed. This time... I don't think it will.

choices.text: [INT 10] Ask about the old garrison cache.

ember_war.text: "The first time the Veil cracked. Fire rained for weeks. We sealed it with blood and prayer." His hands shake as he speaks.

cache.text: "Aye, you've done your reading. There was a supply cache we never emptied — under the old milestone." He draws you a map.

how_stop.text: "Find the source. Close it. Or kill whatever's keeping it open. Easier said than done." He drops a bent coin on the table as you go.

leave.text: "Stay sharp out there, soldier." He turns back to his empty mug.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A stone tablet, half-buried. The inscription is in a language that predates the kingdom.

choices.text: [STR 12] Pry the tablet up — something glinted beneath.

translate.text: "When the veil between worlds thins to nothing, the Sovereign shall wake." Chilling.

partial.text: You catch fragments: "veil," "sovereign," "wake." Not enough to be sure of the meaning.

copy.text: The symbols are intricate. Someone scholarly might make sense of them.

pry.text: Stone scrapes stone. Beneath the tablet: a burial offering — old silver, wrapped around a pale ring.

leave.text: The tablet shifts then slams down on your fingers. You leave empty-handed and bruised.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I died here. A long time ago. The veil won't let me pass through.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I remember... a fortress. A seal. Something that held the fire back. It's broken now, isn't it?

truth.text: "Then it's all happening again. I wish I could help. I wish I could do anything at all." He fades slightly.

fortress.text: "South. Through the ash fields. It was called the Obsidian Bastion. If anything remains, the seal might be restored."

release.text: You speak words of passage. He smiles — truly smiles — and dissolves into light.

trapped.text: "Thank you for trying. No one else has." He fades but remains, caught between worlds.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A vast mural painted on the cave wall. It shows the Veil as a living thing — a membrane between two realities, pulsing like a heart.

choices.text: [DEX 16] Chip a loose fresco fragment free.

study.text: The mural shows a figure sealing the Veil — and another figure tearing it open. Two sides of the same conflict, repeated across ages.

hidden.text: Behind the paint: a second layer. A map. It shows the location of an ancient seal — still intact.

fragment.text: A palm-sized shard of the mural comes free cleanly. The pigment still hums with latent Veil-craft — a fine scholar's prize.

crumble.text: Your chisel slips. The fragment dusts into your eyes and you stagger back, half-blind.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Our village is gone. The fire came in the night — but it wasn't natural fire. It moved with purpose.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Whatever you're hunting out there... please. End it before more villages burn.

choices.text: What did the fire look like?

promise.text: She clutches your hand. "Then there is still hope." The weight of her faith settles on your shoulders.

describe.text: "It had eyes. The flames had eyes." She shudders. "And they watched us run."

donate.text: Tears fill her eyes. "Bless you. This will feed the children for a week."

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Shelves of charred books. Most are ash, but a few tomes survived in a sealed alcove.

find.text: A treatise on Veil magic. The theory is complex, but the practical applications are clear.

dust.text: The books crumble at your touch. Centuries of knowledge, gone in a puff of ash.

take.text: You grab three intact volumes. Their titles are in a dead language, but the diagrams speak clearly.

leave.text: The library creaks ominously. Best not linger.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The... the garrison... fell. The commander is dead. The seal is... broken...

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: He's barely alive. Whatever message he carried, it cost him everything to deliver it.

heal_him.text: You bind his wounds. He'll live, but he can't go further. "Thank you... warn the capital..."

ask_seal.text: "The Obsidian Seal. It held the Veil shut. Something broke it from the inside." He passes out.

take.text: His dispatches detail troop movements and a terrible loss. The information is valuable.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Sit! Rest! I've stories from the four corners of this burning world.

choices.text: [INT 10] Match his last verse with one of your own.

listen.text: He tells of a hero from the last age who sealed the Veil with her own life. "They say her sword still burns, somewhere deep."

emberveil.text: "The Emberveil is a wound that refuses to scar. Each generation thinks they've healed it. Each generation is wrong."

duet.text: His eyes light up. The fire leans in. By the third verse, coins clatter into his hat — and he splits the take with you.

leave.text: You fumble the meter. He winces. "Stick to the sword, friend." You slink off.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A monolith covered in text that writes itself. New words appear as you watch, steaming on the stone.

choices.text: [STR 16] Press your palm to the searing script.

read.text: "The one who walks between will face the Sovereign in the place where time began." Cryptic but urgent.

interpret.text: The prophecy speaks of you — or someone like you. The Sovereign awaits at the Shattered Core. This is your path.

confused.text: The words shift faster than you can read. A headache blooms behind your eyes.

brand.text: The stone brands a single glyph into your palm. It does not hurt — it remembers. Old power flows up your arm.

burned.text: The heat is too much. You pull back with blistered fingers.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Voices. Coming from everywhere and nowhere. They speak your name.

listen.text: They tell you things — true things — about the world before the Veil. Knowledge pours in like water. It hurts.

block.text: Silence. Blessed, deafening silence. The whispers withdraw, offended.

overwhelm.text: Too many voices. Your nose bleeds. When they finally stop, you're on your knees.

respond.text: "We know you." "We've been waiting." "Come closer." You decide not to.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A grave marker, recently placed. "Here fell Kael Ironforge, who stood when others ran." Flowers wilt in the heat.

respect.text: You stand in silence. The wind carries the faint sound of a soldier's hymn.

search.text: Beneath the marker: a letter and a few coins. The letter begs someone named Alira to flee north.

leave.text: Another name for the dead. Too many to count.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Stars float around you — not above, but everywhere. A three-dimensional map of realities, each star a universe.

choices.text: [INT 18] Study the pattern of the Void.
[DEX 18] Pluck a dim star from the constellation.

find_home.text: There — a faint, flickering star. Yours. It's dimming. The Veil is consuming it.

study.text: The Void isn't chaos. It's a pattern. A hunger with geometry. Understanding dawns — and with it, terror.

lost.text: The stars spin. You lose all sense of direction. When it stops, you're exactly where you started.

pluck.text: A dying star comes loose between thumb and forefinger. Cold as thought, heavy as an oath. You pocket it.

burned.text: The star flares white. Starfire races up your arm before you can let go.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A circle of standing stones. They hum — a low, resonant tone that vibrates in your chest.

choices.text: [STR 12] Strike a stone to make it ring.

touch.text: A vision: the forest before the corruption. Green, alive, full of light. It fades, leaving warmth in its place.

pattern.text: The stones sing in the old tongue. You catch a phrase: "The seed of the world sleeps beneath the deepest root."

noise.text: Just noise. Beautiful, but meaningless to you.

ring.text: The struck stone peals and the circle answers. A hidden hollow opens at the centerstone, spilling out old druid-offerings.

crack.text: You hit the stone wrong and the shock rattles up your arm. The song sours.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: They chained me here for speaking truth. The Sovereign fears truth more than swords.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I will tell you what I told them, if you free me. Or leave me here to rot. Your choice.

free.text: "The Sovereign was human once. Before the Veil consumed them. Remember that when you face them." It vanishes in smoke.

listen_only.text: "Clever. The Sovereign has a weakness — it remembers being afraid. Use that." It strains against the chains but cannot follow.

leave.text: It howls as you walk away. "FOOLS! YOU'LL FACE THE SOVEREIGN BLIND!"

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A goblin lies in the road, clutching a wound. It looks up at you with genuine fear.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Please... no more hurt. Skrag just want go home.

choices.text: Kill it. Goblins are the enemy.

help.text: You bind its wound. It stares at you, confused. "Why help Skrag?" Then it limps away, glancing back.

kill.text: It doesn't resist. The light leaves its eyes quickly. You feel nothing. That might be the problem.

leave.text: You step around it. Its whimpers follow you down the road.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A goblin raiding party camps nearby. Their water comes from this well. You have poison — enough to taint it.

choices.text: Poison the well. End the raids.
Destroy the well instead. Let them move on.
Leave the well. Fight them face to face.

poison.text: You pour the vial. By morning, the raids will stop. So will the goblins. All of them — warriors, children, elders.

destroy.text: You collapse the well with a boulder. Without water, they'll have to move on. Less lethal, but they'll raid elsewhere.

leave.text: You'll face them with steel, not subterfuge. It's harder, but you can live with it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: One soul to seal the breach. One life to save thousands. The mathematics is simple. The choice is not.

refuse.text: "Then the breach widens. You have chosen. Live with it." The voice falls silent.

self_sacrifice.text: The breach flares — then something pulls you back. "Not you. Not yet. You have more to do." The breach seals on its own.

challenge.text: "No seal requires a soul. That's a lie of the Veil, meant to feed itself." The voice hisses and vanishes. The breach weakens.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A soldier from your side, caught selling information to the Veil cult. He kneels, bound, awaiting judgment.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: They have my family. I had no choice.

choices.text: Free him. Use him as a double agent.
Let him go. But banish him.

execute.text: He closes his eyes. You make it quick. The others watch in silence. Morale hardens.

double_agent.text: "Feed them false information. Earn your family's freedom. Betray me again and there won't be a third chance."

banish.text: He stumbles away. Whether he returns to the cult or flees north, you may never know.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: A sickness spreads through our village. The healer says it's the Veil's doing — a slow corruption.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: She can cure it, but needs a component found only in the goblin shaman's lair. Will you retrieve it?

choices.text: Agree to help. We'll find the component.
The village may already be lost.

help.text: "Thank the gods. The lair is east, past the spider hollow." He presses gold into your hand.

refuse.text: His face crumbles. "Then we are already dead." He turns away.

investigate.text: The healer's ingredients match Veil cult reagents. She may be making things worse. You confront her — she flees into the night.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The goblins are coming across the bridge. If we burn it, the village is safe — but twenty people on the other side are trapped.

choices.text: Burn the bridge. Save the village.
Hold the bridge. Buy time for everyone.
[STR 14] Collapse the bridge partially — passable for people, not goblins.

burn.text: The bridge roars with flame. The village cheers. Across the river, screams.

hold.text: You stand on the bridge, blade in hand. The goblins hesitate. The refugees cross. Then you fight.

partial.text: You hack through the support beams at precise angles. The bridge sags — too narrow for a charge, but civilians squeeze across one by one.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Please... I haven't eaten in three days. Even a coin. Just one.

give.text: Tears stream down filthy cheeks. "Thank you, thank you." The coin vanishes into rags.

food.text: You share your rations. The child eats like it might be their last meal. It might be.

leave.text: You can't save everyone. You repeat that until it almost sounds true.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I offer power. Genuine, immediate, devastating power. The cost? A memory. One you hold dear.

accept.text: Power floods through you. Something precious fades — you can't remember what. That's the point.

refuse.text: "Your loss. The Sovereign won't offer such gentle terms." It dissolves into shadow.

negotiate.text: "Clever mortal. Fine — power for pain. No memories taken." Fire courses through you.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: My leg's broken. I can't walk. The ash wraiths are close — I can hear them.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Leave me a sword. I'll buy you time.

choices.text: Carry them. We leave no one behind.
[STR 14] Splint the leg. They walk or we both die.

carry.text: The extra weight slows you. The wraiths catch up. You fight carrying them on your back.

leave_sword.text: They take the blade with shaking hands. "Go. I'll hold as long as I can." You don't hear the fight end.

splint.text: You fashion a splint from debris. They lean on you, limping, but alive. Together, you outpace the wraiths.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: That relic you carry — it belongs to this temple. It was stolen generations ago.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Return it, and the temple's blessing is yours. Keep it, and carry the weight of theft.

choices.text: Keep it. We need it more.

return.text: She takes it reverently. Light blooms from the altar. "Be blessed, honorable one."

keep.text: "Then you are no better than the thief who took it." Her disappointment is heavier than any curse.

share.text: "An arrangement. Unconventional." She considers. "Very well. The temple will empower it for your use — if you return it after your quest."

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The fire crackles. Someone brings up the question everyone's been avoiding: what happens after?

choices.text: "There is no after. There's only the next fight."

rest.text: Nods around the fire. For now, that's enough.

grim.text: Silence. Someone stirs the embers. The mood darkens.

hope.text: A faint smile from the group. "Something better. Yeah. I'd fight for that."

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The party pools their rations. It's not much, but sharing makes it taste better.

choices.text: Give your portion to the wounded.

share.text: Laughter, for the first time in days. It doesn't last, but it matters.

give.text: They protest, but eat. Your stomach growls, but their gratitude warms you.

silent.text: Everyone eats. Nobody speaks. Sometimes that's enough.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I found this in your pack when I was looking for bandages. Care to explain?

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: They hold up a Veil cult symbol. You've never seen it before. Someone planted it.

choices.text: "I took it from a dead cultist. For study."

deny.text: They stare at you. "I want to believe that." The tension lingers.

explain.text: "...That makes sense. Sorry. These days, you can't trust anyone." The air clears.

counter.text: "Why were you searching my pack?" They flush. "Point taken. Let's drop it."

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: You wake to screaming. One of your companions thrashes in their bedroll, caught in a nightmare.

choices.text: [INT 12] The dream might be a Veil intrusion.

wake.text: They gasp, eyes wild. "The fire... I was in the fire..." You sit with them until dawn.

sleep.text: The screaming stops eventually. In the morning, they don't remember.

veil_dream.text: You recognize the signs. You touch their forehead and push the Veil influence out. They sleep peacefully after.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I can't do this. I'm not brave. I'm not a hero. I'm just... I'm just scared.

choices.text: "So am I. That's what courage is."
"Then go. I won't force anyone to die."

courage.text: They swallow hard. Nod. Grip their weapon tighter. They stay.

let_go.text: They leave before dawn. Their bedroll is still warm when you wake.

need.text: "You... need me?" Something shifts in their eyes. Resolve. "Okay. Okay, I'll stay."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I wasn't always a fighter. I was a baker. In a village that doesn't exist anymore.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Veil took everything. My family. My home. My flour stores.

choices.text: "We'll make them pay for what they took."
"When this is over, you can bake again."

vengeance.text: Their jaw sets. "Every Veil cultist I cut down, I think of bread dough. Same motion." Dark humor, but genuine.

remember.text: They talk for an hour. The village had a festival every autumn. The children loved their honey rolls. They cry, quietly.

hope.text: "Bake again..." They laugh, surprised. "I'd like that. I'd really like that."

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Two of your companions are at each other's throats. Something about who should take point in the next fight.

mediate.text: They grudgingly agree to alternate. Not perfect, but the peace holds.

fight.text: Three punches later, they're both winded and somehow friends again. Violence: the universal language.

rank.text: "I decide. End of discussion." They fall in line. Respect or resentment — hard to tell.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: One of your companions begins humming — and the campfire flares. They have magic they haven't told you about.

choices.text: "How long have you been able to do that?"

ask.text: "Since the Veil cracked. Something... woke up inside me. I was afraid you'd think I was corrupted."

demonstrate.text: They extend a hand. A flame dances on each fingertip. "It's not much. But it's mine."

confront.text: "Because the last person who saw me do this called me a monster and ran." Fair point.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I've written a letter. For my daughter. In case I don't...

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Will you carry it? If the worst happens?

choices.text: "I'll carry it. But you'll deliver it yourself."
"Write her two. One for if you make it."

promise.text: They smile. "That's what I needed to hear." They fold the letter away.

accept.text: The letter is heavier than paper should be. You tuck it into your armor, close to your heart.

two_letters.text: They laugh, surprised. "Two letters. Yeah. Yeah, I like that." They write both. The second one is longer.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A companion challenges you to a sparring match. "Come on, we're getting soft sitting around camp."

choices.text: Too tired. Rest is more valuable.

spar.text: Blunted blades ring across camp. You both land good hits. By the end, you feel sharper.

teach.text: You demonstrate a parry technique from your training. They pick it up fast. "Where did you learn that?"

rest.text: "Fair enough. Tomorrow, then." They practice alone in the firelight.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A loose stone in the wall. Behind it: a leather pouch, heavy with coin.

take.text: Gold. Real gold. Whoever hid this isn't coming back for it.

leave.text: You walk on. Better safe than trapped.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Clear water bubbles from a mossy rock. It glows faintly — natural magic, old and gentle.

drink.text: Warmth spreads through your body. Wounds close. Fatigue lifts.

fill.text: The water stays clear in your skins. It'll keep.

test.text: Pure — and potent. You drink your fill, knowing it's safe. The effect is even stronger when you relax into it.

drink_normal.text: Refreshing, but you tense up. Still healing, just not as much.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A campsite, recently abandoned. Bedrolls, cooking gear, and a locked strongbox left behind in haste.

search.text: Scattered coins, a half-eaten meal, and a map with locations circled in red.

unlock.text: Click. The box opens. Medicine, gold, and a letter addressed to no one.

jammed.text: The lock resists. You settle for loose items around the camp.

food.text: Dried meat and hard bread. Enough for another day on the road.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A merchant and his mule, both dead. Arrows in their backs. The cart is overturned but not fully looted.

choices.text: Bury the dead first, then salvage.
Move on. This road is dangerous.

salvage.text: Bolts of cloth, some tools, and a pouch of gold hidden under the seat.

bury.text: You take time to bury them. Under the cart: their emergency fund. They'd want someone to use it.

leave.text: The arrows are goblin-made. This isn't a place to linger.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A grove of bioluminescent mushrooms. Some are edible — some are very much not.

eat.text: They taste like earth and honey. Your stomach holds. Barely.

identify.text: The blue-capped ones are powerful restoratives. You harvest carefully.

wrong_ones.text: You pick the red ones. Your vision swims for an hour.

leave.text: Pretty, but you're not hungry enough to gamble.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A mine entrance, timbers rotting. Ore veins glint in the first few feet of darkness.

choices.text: [STR 14] Go deeper for the good stuff.

mine.text: Surface ore. Low quality, but it'll sell.
