deep.text: You haul out a vein of crystallized fire-ore. Rare and valuable.

collapse.text: The timbers groan and buckle. You barely escape the cave-in.

leave.text: That mine has been waiting to collapse for years. Not today.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A supply wagon lies on its side. Crates are scattered across the road.

open.text: Rations, medicine, and a small amount of gold. Standard military supplies.

survivors.text: A driver, unconscious but alive. You wake him. "Thank you — take whatever you need from the cargo."

careful.text: You approach cautiously. No ambush — just an accident. You take what's useful.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Heat-formed crystals jut from the rock face. They pulse with inner fire — ember gems.

harvest.text: Three small gems, warm to the touch. They'll fetch a good price.

careful.text: You extract a perfect cluster. It radiates warmth and light.

shatter.text: The crystal shatters. Shards cut your hands. You salvage a few fragments.

leave.text: Beautiful but not worth bleeding for.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Gold coins scattered at the edge of a lava pool. The heat is intense, but the gold is real.

choices.text: Use a stick to rake them in.

grab.text: Quick hands, singed hair. You come away with a fistful of gold coins.

burn.text: The heat is worse than you thought. You snatch a few coins but pay in blisters.

rake.text: Slow but effective. You pull a dozen coins to safety.

leave.text: Gold isn't worth much to a corpse.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A crystal floating in empty space. It's perfectly black — not reflecting light, but absorbing it.

touch.text: Cold. Absolute cold. When you release it, your hand is numb — but the crystal follows you now.

analyze.text: It's crystallized void energy. Incredibly dangerous, incredibly valuable. You contain it safely.

touch_bad.text: It bites. Reality shudders. You drop it and nurse a hand that briefly didn't exist.

leave.text: It watches you leave. Crystals shouldn't be able to watch. This one does.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A tear in the air. Through it: a landscape of impossible geometry. Something on the other side sees you.

reach.text: Something grabs your hand — then releases it. When you pull back, you're holding a coin that weighs nothing.

seal.text: You weave the ambient energy and stitch the rift closed. The air sighs with relief.

backlash.text: The rift fights back. Energy lashes your mind.

leave.text: You back away. The rift watches you go, then slowly closes on its own. For now.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: You blink and you're somewhere else. A throne room. A figure on the throne, wreathed in fire. It speaks your name.

approach.text: The figure reaches out. "You will come to me. Eventually." You wake with a burn mark on your palm.

demand.text: "Who are you?" "What you will become. Or what will destroy you." The dream shatters.

wake.text: You tear yourself free. The throne room crumbles. You wake gasping. It felt too real.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A sword, driven into a stone altar. It hums with dark energy. The blade seems to drink the light around it.

pull.text: The blade slides free. Power surges up your arm — intoxicating and wrong. You keep it.

examine.text: A hunger enchantment — it feeds on the wielder. You disable the curse first, then take the blade safely.

pull_bad.text: The blade bites your hand as you draw it. It feeds. You drop it, weaker.

leave.text: Some swords are better left in their stones.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: You turn a corner and see... yourself. From five minutes ago. Walking the path you just walked.

choices.text: Close your eyes and keep walking.

follow.text: Your echo leads you to something you missed — a hidden alcove with treasure you walked right past.

break_loop.text: You step out of the loop. Time snaps back to normal. The echo waves goodbye and fades.

trapped.text: You walk the same path three times before realizing. Lost time, lost energy.

ignore.text: When you open your eyes, the echo is gone. Time flows normally. Probably.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Ha! Mortals! Stomping through my forest with your iron boots and serious faces!

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Play a game with me. Win and I give you a prize. Lose and I take your left sock.

play.text: It's a riddle. You get it wrong, but the sprite is delighted by your answer anyway. "Close enough! Have a shiny!"

cheat.text: You outwit a fey creature. It's impressed. "Clever! Have TWO shinies!"

caught.text: "CHEATER! CHEATER!" It steals your sock and vanishes cackling. Your foot is cold now.

leave.text: "Boring! BORING!" It throws an acorn at your head and disappears.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A still pool of water, perfectly reflective. Your reflection looks back — but it's not mimicking you. It moves on its own.

touch.text: Your reflection grabs your hand and pulls. You resist. It lets go, smiling. Something gold remains in your palm.

speak.text: "You're running out of time," it says. "The Sovereign knows you're coming." It dissolves into ripples.

understand.text: A Veil reflection — a window to the other side. Your mirror self is an echo. It hands you a gift through the water.

touch_bad.text: The water burns. Your reflection laughs and you yank your hand free.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I am looking for my grave. I have been looking for a very long time.

choices.text: [INT 12] Guide them by reading the ley lines.

help.text: You search for an hour. You find a collapsed cairn. "Thank you." The spirit sinks peacefully into the earth.

guide.text: The ley lines pulse under your feet. You lead the spirit straight to its resting place. It gifts you its last possession.

lost.text: The ley lines are tangled here. You walk in circles. The spirit sighs. "Another day, then."

leave.text: It watches you go with hollow eyes. "Perhaps another traveler..."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I have stood here since before your kingdom had a name. I have a question for you, mortal.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Is the world worth saving? Answer honestly. I will know if you lie.

choices.text: "I don't know. But I'm trying anyway."
"No. But the people in it are."

yes.text: "Certain. Good. Certainty is a weapon." The statue crumbles, and from its heart falls a jewel.

honest.text: "Honesty. Rarer than gold. Here." A crack appears in its chest. Inside: a gem of pure light.

people.text: "The wisest answer. The world is stone and fire. But the people — the people burn brighter." It gifts you its core.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Colors that don't exist in nature paint the void. They form shapes — faces, places, moments that haven't happened yet.

watch.text: You see yourself at the end. Standing before the Sovereign. Blade raised. The vision fades before you see who wins.

alter.text: You reach into the vision and shift a detail — a door opens that was closed. The future trembles and reforms.

watch_bad.text: The vision twists. You see yourself losing. The Sovereign's laugh echoes. Is it real, or is the Void lying?

leave.text: The future will come regardless. You'd rather face it fresh.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A sound. Not heard — felt. The frequency of creation itself, the note that began everything. It fills you completely.

flow.text: For one eternal moment, you understand everything. Then it fades, leaving only warmth and peace.

harmonize.text: You sing back. The universe listens. In that shared note: power beyond reckoning. You carry a fragment of it now.

discord.text: Your note clashes. The sound recoils. Everything hurts.

shield.text: You block it out. Safe, but diminished. You sense you missed something important.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Every road leads somewhere. Mine leads to penance.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I killed a man who deserved to live. I walk until I find someone worth saving.

choices.text: Wish him well and move on.

walk.text: He says little, but when the road forks he points you down the safer path. "Goblins that way. Took me a week to learn that."

ask.text: "A healer. Thought he was a spy. The fever killed three others before I understood." He won't meet your eyes. You understand silence.

leave.text: "Safe roads," he says, and means it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I've been mapping these roads for forty years.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Lost my eyes in the Veil breach at Korrath. Gained something better. I feel the land now.

choices.text: Ask her about the roads ahead.
Buy a map from her (15 gold).
[INT 12] Ask about the Veil breach at Korrath.

ask_roads.text: "The dust road narrows three miles ahead. Ash wraith territory. Stick to the eastern ditch — they hate the shade."

buy_map.text: She hands you a map sketched in impossibly precise detail. "Feel the paper. The raised lines are cliffs."

korrath.text: "Korrath. Four hundred souls. The Veil didn't just crack there — it spoke." She taps her empty eye sockets. "It told me what was coming. I wish I'd been wrong."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I challenge one person per road. For sport. The loser buys the wine.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Don't look so worried. I haven't killed anyone in... months.

accept.text: The blade is fast — faster than expected. You hold your own. At first blood, they bow. "Acceptable. Better than most." They press gold into your hand.

disarm.text: Their sword skitters across the road. The mask tilts. A laugh from behind it. "Oh, I like you." They pay double.

cut.text: They were already moving. A shallow line across your forearm. "Too slow. Ten gold for the lesson."

decline.text: "Wise or cowardly. Hard to tell from behind that face." They saunter off, spinning their blade.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I am — was — a lord. Now I carry my own bags. It's surprisingly educational.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: My cousin took the title. With poison. I have nowhere to go and too much pride to beg.

choices.text: Ask what he knows of the nobility.

share.text: He eats with undisguised relief. "I haven't had a hot meal in three days. I... thank you. Genuinely."

ask.text: "The garrison at Thornwall has a new commander. Bought the post. Incompetent. The south gate has no night watch." Useful.

recruit.text: "A purpose. I hadn't considered that." He straightens. "I know supply routes, tax records, and where every lord in three provinces buries their secrets." He joins your cause.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Veil sickness spreads faster than I can document it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The beak mask isn't affectation. The herbs inside filter the corruption. Mostly.

ask.text: "Three stages. First the dreams. Then the markings. Then they stop being entirely here." She taps her ledger. "I'm on page four hundred and seven."

buy.text: She hands you a packet of dried herbs. "Breathe through this in corrupted zones. May add a day or two."

collaborate.text: You compare notes. She finds a pattern neither of you had spotted alone. "This changes everything." She presses her entire herb supply into your hands.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The ground gives way to mossy steps descending into a flooded temple. Old — far older than anything in the history books.

choices.text: Seal it back up. Whatever sleeps here should stay sleeping.

wade.text: Thigh-deep in cold black water, your torch reflects off a submerged altar. Something glints beneath the surface. You pull out a sealed reliquary.

read_carvings.text: The carvings are pre-Veil. A warning: "The god here died willingly so the Veil could not consume it." Inside, safe by virtue of its sacrifice: offerings left untouched for centuries.

slip.text: The steps are slick. You go under. The cold is stunning. You scramble out empty-handed and soaked.

leave.text: The moss closes back over the steps as you watch. Agreeable ruins.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Ten thousand names, carved into a wall that's slowly sinking into the earth. A battle you don't recognize. A war no one remembers.

choices.text: [INT 12] Identify the battle from context clues.
Take a rubbing of a section.

read.text: Thousands of names. Some still legible. One catches your eye — the same family name as someone you know. A cold coincidence.

identify.text: "The Hollowing." A battle fought inside the Veil itself, four hundred years ago. The survivors reportedly went mad. None of the histories mention it. Why not?

rubbing.text: You press paper to stone and charcoal across it. The names transfer. You fold it carefully. Someone should remember them.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: HALT. THIS FACILITY IS SEALED BY ORDER OF THE THIRD CONCORDAT. STATE YOUR AUTHORIZATION.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The brass automaton has been standing here for at least two centuries. Its eyes still glow orange.

bluff.text: "AUTHORIZATION UNRECOGNIZED. REROUTING TO MANUAL REVIEW." It stands perfectly still, waiting. You walk around it. It tracks you but doesn't stop you.

codes.text: "CONCORDAT SEAL ACKNOWLEDGED. PROCEED, ARCHIVIST." The door behind it swings open. Inside: a vault that hasn't been touched in two hundred years.

destroy.text: The head clangs off the wall. The body collapses. Inside the chest cavity: a power crystal. Still warm.

denied.text: "UNAUTHORIZED PERSONNEL. INITIATING DETERRENT." Lightning crackles from its fingertips.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A tower, half-collapsed. At the top, visible through the ruin: a telescope the size of a war horse, pointed at the sky.

climb.text: You reach the top. Through the telescope, the sky looks wrong — too many stars, in the wrong positions. One of them blinks.

search.text: The ground floor yields a logbook. The last entry: "It sees us looking. We must stop looking." Three days before the collapse.

fall.text: A rotten beam gives way. You catch yourself two floors down. Bruised, wiser.

leave.text: The tower groans as if disappointed.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Every surface is covered in writing. Not carved — grown. Living script, slowly spreading across the stone.

choices.text: Burn it. Knowledge that grows on its own is dangerous.

read.text: The script rearranges itself for you. A complete history of the Veil's formation — who opened it, and why. The truth is worse than you imagined.

touch.text: The word you touch — "sovereign" — burns into your fingertip. The script seethes. You yank your hand back.

overwhelm.text: The script floods your mind faster than you can process. You stagger out, head ringing, carrying fragments of terrible knowledge.

burn.text: The fire doesn't catch. The script simply writes over the scorched stone in minutes. Whatever this is, it doesn't die easily.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A massive cave bear sits motionless over a smaller shape. A cub. Cold. The bear doesn't growl — it just breathes, slow and heavy.

choices.text: [INT 10] Check if the cub is truly dead.

leave.text: Some losses are not yours to fix. You walk quietly past.

check.text: The cub stirs. Alive — barely. You apply what little medicine you have. The bear watches without moving. When the cub whimpers, the bear exhales, long and deep.

food.text: You set your rations down twenty feet away and back off. The bear doesn't move, but by morning the food is gone.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A fox trots across your path. It casts two shadows in different directions. Neither matches its movement.

follow.text: It leads you through a path that shouldn't exist — a shortcut through the Veil-twisted terrain. At the end, it sits and waits. Then vanishes.

understand.text: A Veil echo — a creature that exists in two states simultaneously. One shadow is from our world, one from the other side. Following it is safe. It's a guide, of sorts.

leave.text: It pauses, looks back over its shoulder, then continues into the dark. Something about those eyes stays with you.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A tortoise the size of a table. Its shell is carved with runes — not by any human hand. It watches you with tremendous patience.

choices.text: [INT 12] Read the runes on its shell.

sit.text: You sit. It regards you. The world is quieter here. When you leave, you feel oddly rested.

read.text: Names. Hundreds of names etched in spiraling script. Travelers, probably. Everyone who ever sat with it. You find yourself wanting to add yours.

feed.text: It eats from your hand with meditative slowness. In return it allows you to rest against its shell, which radiates gentle warmth.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Moths the color of starless sky descend and cover you from shoulder to knee. They don't bite. They just... rest.

choices.text: Stay still and let them settle.

still.text: They pulse gently. Where they touch you, the ache from old wounds fades. When they lift, you're lighter than before.

brush.text: They scatter without malice. On the ground where they were: a fine silver dust. It dissolves when you try to collect it.

listen.text: The hum of their wings carries a message — not words, but sensation. A location. A name. The Sovereign's true name, spoken in the void.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A hawk circles, trailing electricity. It lands on a rock nearby and tilts its head at you. It seems to be waiting.

choices.text: [DEX 12] Catch it to examine the lightning around it.

arm.text: It steps onto your arm. A tingle of electricity runs up to your shoulder — sharp but not painful. It stares at the horizon, then launches. A message, delivered in feeling: storm coming from the east.

catch.text: Your hands close around it gently. Its feathers crackle. You examine the lightning — it's Veil energy, converted harmlessly by the hawk's body. Remarkable.

shocked.text: The hawk objects to being grabbed. Thirty volts of objection. You both retreat with dignity.

watch.text: It watches back. Eventually loses interest and flies east. You watch it go with a feeling you can't name.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A lantern hanging from a branch, unlit. When you approach, it lights on its own — black flame, cold, casting shadows that point the wrong way.

take.text: The lantern comes with you. That night, it shows you paths invisible to normal light. It also shows you things following you. That information has a cost.

inspect.text: A soul-binder. Someone's spirit powers the flame — probably the previous carrier's. You dismantle the binding carefully. The flame dies. A faint thank-you drifts past.

take_bad.text: The moment you grip it, cold floods your arm. You drop it, shaking. Your hand is grey for an hour.

smash.text: It shatters. Black flame sputters and dies. A whisper: "...finally." Whether gratitude or threat, you can't tell.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A gold coin in the road. You almost pass it. But it's warm — too warm for something that's been lying in the dirt.

pick_up.text: It feels like it wants to be held. Three times that day you find gold where there shouldn't be any. Three times your dreams are red. Worth it? Maybe.

examine.text: It's been cursed to attract its carrier to danger. Every piece of gold it brings you is bait. You kick it into the ditch.

leave.text: You walk on. Later, you hear footsteps behind you — someone else picked it up.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A shard of mirror, half-buried. Your reflection in it doesn't move when you do. It's just standing there, watching.

speak.text: "You're going to fail," it says, in your voice. "Not today. But eventually." It offers no comfort when you ask when. It just smiles with your face.

bind.text: You seal the shard with a word of binding. The reflection hammers against the glass once, then stills. The shard is now an incredibly powerful scrying lens.

escape.text: You try to contain it. The reflection steps halfway out before you shove it back in. The shard cracks. "Next time," it says with your voice.

bury.text: You cover it with earth. Three feet down. As you walk away, you hear muffled knocking from below.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A gauntlet on a battlefield relic. Perfectly preserved. When the wind blows, it hums — a warrior's death song.

choices.text: [STR 14] Crush it before it bonds to someone.
[INT 12] Identify the warrior whose spirit remains.

wear.text: It fits perfectly. In battle, your strikes become faster, more certain. But between fights, your hand moves on its own sometimes. Reaching for a weapon. Reaching for a throat.

crush.text: The steel buckles. The humming becomes a shriek, then silence. Whatever was inside it is free now. Hopefully at peace.

snaps_on.text: As you grip it to test it, it locks onto your wrist. Tight. Humming.

identify.text: "General Voss of the third cohort. Died alone on this field." You speak her name aloud. The humming stops. The gauntlet is simply excellent steel now.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A book, open on a rock. Writing is filling the pages right now, with no hand guiding the pen. It's describing you. Your journey. It's almost caught up to the present.

choices.text: Read what it says about your future.
[INT 14] Understand what kind of curse this is.

read_future.text: The pages ahead describe battles you haven't fought, choices you haven't made. You read to the last written page. It ends mid-sentence: "And then she—"

tear.text: The pages tear. The book screams — a paper sound, wrong and high. The writing on the remaining pages begins frantically rewriting itself.

understand.text: A fate-binding tome. Someone commissioned it to trap you in a predetermined story. You write a single word in the margin: "No." The book slams shut and won't reopen.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The sky turns the color of bruised flesh. Rain begins — black as ink, smelling of copper and char.

choices.text: Press through it — you're close to your destination.

shelter.text: Under an overhang, you watch the black rain strip bark from trees. Nothing grows where it falls. You wait it out intact.

collect.text: You bottle a sample without skin contact. Concentrated Veil essence — volatile, but with careful handling, a potent reagent.

soak.text: The rain finds the gaps in your armor. It burns like salt in a wound. You emerge stained and weakened.

press.text: You make it. But the black rain leaves marks on your skin that take a day to fade. And the dreams that night are not yours.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Fog so thick you cannot see your hand. And silence — complete silence. No wind. No insects. Even your footsteps seem muffled.

choices.text: [INT 10] Read the ley lines through the fog.
[DEX 12] Stay low and feel for the road.

memory.text: Your memory holds. Every turn correct. When the fog lifts, you're where you meant to be — but an hour has passed that you cannot account for.

ley.text: The ley lines pulse faintly underfoot, steady as a compass needle. You emerge into clear air with your bearings intact.

feel.text: Your fingers find the road's stone edge. You follow it, slow but sure. In the fog, something brushes past you. You don't stop.

lost.text: The fog disorients you. You walk for an hour before the sun burns through. You've come full circle, back to your starting point.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A wall of fire on the horizon, moving. Too fast for the wind. It's being pushed.

choices.text: Find a cave and seal the entrance.

outrun.text: Your lungs burn but your legs burn harder and you beat it. Behind you, the land turns to glass.

firebreak.text: You spot the old burn scar — a wide strip of bare earth from last year's fire. You cross it and watch the new firestorm devour itself at the border.

caught.text: The edge catches you. You roll, smother the flames, and stagger forward into clean air. Singed, but alive.

cave.text: You seal the mouth with loose rocks. The roar passes overhead. When you emerge, the world outside is burnt flat but you are whole.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Lightning rises from the ground. Rain falls upward. The clouds below your feet flicker with inverted thunder.

choices.text: Stand in it and feel what it does.
[INT 16] Draw the energy into something useful.

stand.text: The inverted rain washes over you. It doesn't wet. It clarifies. For a moment, you see every Veil rift within a mile, mapped in your mind.

shield.text: You cross the storm quickly. Your armor absorbs a few rising strikes. You emerge dented but intact.

draw.text: You funnel the inverted lightning into a focus point — the energy stabilizes, concentrated. Held in your palm like a second heartbeat.

discharge.text: The energy grounds through you instead. Every hair on your body stands up. You taste copper for a day.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Snow falls that shouldn't — it's not cold enough. Each flake dissolves on touch and leaves an image: a face, a place, a moment. Someone's memories, scattered to the wind.

choices.text: Catch the flakes and watch the memories.

watch.text: A life plays out in fragments. A child's birthday. A first love. A burning house. Someone's entire existence, dispersed into weather.

trace.text: The memories belong to Elara of Thornwatch — reported missing two months ago. The last memory: the Veil taking her. You know where to look now.

ignore.text: You keep your eyes down. Some griefs aren't yours to carry.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Hold! Who approaches the garrison of Thornwall in the name of the Third King?

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The Third King ruled three centuries ago. The soldier doesn't notice the ruins around him.

choices.text: Play along. "A friend of the kingdom."
Tell him the truth — the kingdom is gone.

play_along.text: "Pass, friend. Watch for the goblin scouts to the east." He stands aside, rigid and proud. You walk through him without resistance.

truth.text: His expression fractures. "The kingdom is... gone?" He looks at his hands — through them. "How long?" He weeps, and then fades.

dismiss.text: "Be at peace, soldier. Your watch is over. The kingdom endures in better forms." He straightens. Salutes. Dissolves.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You can see me.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: He's still out there. The man who did this. He walks free in Thornwall, selling grain and waving to neighbors.

choices.text: Offer to pass her message to the magistrate.

name.text: "Hessler. Hessler the miller." She burns with cold fury. "Remember it." You will.

message.text: "They won't listen. They never do. But... thank you for trying." She hands you her ring — evidence. "Maybe you'll have better luck."

leave.text: She watches you walk away. The cold follows you for three miles.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Don't go that way. The bad thing lives that way.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A child — transparent, cold, no older than eight. She points down the left fork.

choices.text: Trust her. Take the right fork.
Ask what the bad thing is.

trust.text: The right fork is longer but safe. In the distance, you hear sounds from the left path — something large and angry. She was right.

ask.text: "Teeth that don't stop. And eyes. Too many eyes." She won't say more. She fades when you press her.

left.text: You find what she meant. It finds you first. You survive, barely, and take the right fork going back.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I built this fortress. Every stone. Forty years of work.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: And then they told me what it was really for.

choices.text: "What did you do when you found out?"

ask.text: "A Veil anchor. The whole structure channels Veil energy upward. It's why I can't leave. It's why I won't leave — if I go, the anchor stabilizes fully."

passages.text: He brightens. "Twelve. Here, here, and here—" He traces the walls with a ghostly finger. You memorize every route.

did.text: "I kept building. I didn't know how to stop. There's a kind of courage in stopping." He looks down at his transparent hands. "I never learned it in time."

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Hundreds of spirits, moving in unison, singing a single sustained note. The harmony is perfect. The sound makes your ribs ache.

listen.text: The song is grief and defiance together. Every spirit here died fighting the Veil. They're still fighting — with this song. Something in you aligns with it.

understand.text: A resonance ward — they keep the Veil from spreading further by anchoring the border with collective will. They cannot leave. But while they sing, nothing from the Veil can cross here.

pass.text: The choir parts around you like water. Each face you see looks at peace and terrible in equal measure. You emerge on the other side, inexplicably steadied.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I left the militia three days ago. I know that makes me a criminal. But they were marching us into the Veil-touched zone and calling it a "cleansing operation."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I didn't sign up to be fed to it.

choices.text: Let him pass. His fear is valid.
Report him to the next garrison.
[INT 10] Ask what he knows about the militia's plans.

let_pass.text: He nods, grateful and haunted. "Thank you." He melts into the undergrowth.

report.text: You give the garrison his description. They pay a small reward. Whether they catch him, you don't know.

intel.text: He tells you everything — patrol routes, commander weaknesses, supply depots. A deserter with a grudge is a considerable intelligence asset.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I ran the Ironhand crew for six years. A hundred people worked for me. I was very good at a very bad thing.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Then the Veil took my crew. Every last one. I'm the only survivor. Tell me what to do with that.

choices.text: "Use those skills for something worth using them for."
"There's nothing to do with that. Carry it and move."
[STR 12] "Start by putting down anyone who comes after you."

redirect.text: "Skills worth something." She looks at her hands. "Yeah. Maybe. I know every supply route in the region." She joins your cause.

carry.text: She nods slowly. "Honest." She hands you half her provisions. "I travel lighter than I used to."

fight.text: "That I know how to do." A dark smile. "Someone's always coming after you in my trade. Nice to have company in it."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Ten gold. Per person. For the safety this road has thanks to us.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Don't think of it as robbery. Think of it as... infrastructure.

choices.text: [INT 12] Expose the extortion to the local militia.
"I'd like to see you try collecting."

pay.text: He pockets it with a practiced smile. "Smart traveler. Safe roads ahead." They step aside.

expose.text: You memorize their faces and post descriptions at the next garrison. Three days later, you hear the militia cleaned this road out.

fight_back.text: "Oh, a difficult one." He signals. Four others step from the trees. You negotiate with violence. They don't take the toll. They don't have teeth to count it with.

threaten.text: "Feisty! But we've got four bows on you." He smiles. "Let's try again. Ten gold."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: A militia payroll wagon. Two guards, one driver, forty thousand gold pieces.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I've got the route. I need the muscle. Half and half — nobody gets hurt if they're smart about it.

choices.text: Decline. This isn't your kind of work.
[INT 14] Counter-propose: inform the militia for a reward.

agree.text: The job goes smooth. Nobody gets hurt. You walk away with gold and a reputation you didn't ask for.

decline.text: "Your loss." She fades into an alley. The wagon passes unmolested. Probably.

inform.text: You sell the route to the militia commander. They set an ambush. The payout is generous. Korve doesn't know it was you. Yet.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I'm done. Thirty years of this, and I'm done. Last job is just this one thing — then I'm gone west where the Veil hasn't touched.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I need your help. Nothing violent. Just a distraction.

choices.text: Wish him luck but stay clear.
[DEX 12] Offer to do the actual theft — you're quicker.

help.text: Your distraction works perfectly. He gets what he came for. He presses half the take into your hands without a word and disappears west.

decline.text: "Fair enough. Can't blame you." He tips his hat and walks toward his fate alone.

take_over.text: You move faster and cleaner than he could. He watches with professional respect. "Kid like you shouldn't be wasting time on the straight road." He splits the take three ways — including your skill bonus.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A rough stone shrine with a flame that doesn't go out despite the wind. An offering bowl holds old coins.

choices.text: Take the old coins from the bowl.

offer.text: The flame grows briefly brighter and warm in a way that has nothing to do with heat. Something approves. You carry a warmth in your chest that lasts a day.

steal.text: Eight copper coins. And a burn on your palm that won't heal for three days. Some offerings are watched.

pray.text: The flame doesn't judge. It gives what you put into it — which is nothing and nothing back. But you feel heard.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A shrine so covered in growth that only the silhouette of a figure is visible. The name on the base has been worn completely smooth.

choices.text: Clear the vines and restore it.

restore.text: You clear the growth and the figure emerges — a god of travelers. As you step back, you feel the road a little lighter under your feet.

identify.text: Vaerith, god of crossroads — declared heretical and unmade by the Third King's priests. Still here, quietly. "Still here," you say to the stone. The wind replies.

leave.text: Some things are forgotten for a reason. Or perhaps no reason at all. It's hard to know from the outside.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The altar is recent. The offering is recent. Whatever was given here, it was not coin.

choices.text: Leave it. This is not your affair.

destroy.text: The altar cracks under your heel. A shriek from somewhere below you. The air tastes of ash and relief.

identify.text: The sigils belong to the Veil Sovereign's intermediaries — a mid-tier cult cell. They're active here. You note the location.

leave.text: You walk on. That night you wonder if that was the right call. The wondering doesn't stop.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I've been praying at this shrine for six hours. The god still hasn't answered.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Perhaps it needs a different voice.

choices.text: Gods answer in their own time. Or not at all.

join.text: Two voices. The shrine's flame steadies. It doesn't speak, but something about the road ahead feels known. The priest wipes tears he didn't expect to shed.

advise.text: "An offering of action, not words?" He considers. He does something — you don't watch — and the shrine answers. He shares the blessing with you.

leave.text: "You may be right," he says quietly. "But I'd rather keep trying."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The Veil is not evil. It is honest. It shows you what you are when everything else is stripped away.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I worship what is true. Most people find that disturbing.

choices.text: "Truth and evil are not mutually exclusive."

argue.text: "Ah. A pragmatist." He smiles. "Yes. But at least the Veil doesn't lie about it." You find you have no answer to that.

ask.text: He is quiet for a long time. "It showed me that I am very small, and that small things can still matter enormously." He hands you a veil-touched amulet.

debate.text: The argument is long, sharp, and largely honest. Neither of you wins. Both of you learn something. He donates his rations to you, "for arguing in good faith."

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I can't find my flock. I was watching them and then the fog came and—

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: My mum's going to be so angry. She's always angry when the flock scatters.

choices.text: Walk him home first, then search.
[INT 10] Use the sheep's tracks to find the flock quickly.

help.text: An hour of searching. Twelve sheep behind a hedge, calm as furniture. Tam hugs the lead sheep. His mum isn't angry — she was worried about him.

home_first.text: His mother bursts out crying the moment she sees him. She finds the flock herself by noon. She presses warm bread into your hands.

track.text: You follow the prints to a hollow where all twelve sheep are dozing. Tam is so relieved he gives you his lunch.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I've been gathering in this forest for fifty years. And somehow today I am completely lost.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: My basket is full and my knees hurt and I would very much like to go home.

choices.text: Escort her out of the forest.
[INT 10] Navigate by the moss and sun.

escort.text: An hour of slow walking and her stories about every plant you pass. At the forest edge, she identifies three of your herbs as actually mislabeled — then fixes them. Better than any herbalist.

navigate.text: You orient by shadow and moss growth. She follows, impressed. "Sharp eyes. Here — this corrects inflammation. I have plenty."

directions.text: She listens carefully, nods, and heads off. You hear her humming. She'll probably be fine.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: My unit was ambushed. I ran. I'm not proud of that — I ran.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I don't know if anyone else made it. I don't know where I am.

choices.text: Give him your map and send him north.
[INT 12] Use his patrol route to find survivors.

help.text: You find two others from his unit, alive and hidden. The reunion is wordless — the kind that doesn't need words.

map.text: He takes it with shaking hands. "Thank you. I'll—" He catches himself. "Thank you." He goes north.

survivors.text: His patrol route maps to two known survival points. You find three soldiers, not two. All alive.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I was following my master and got distracted by a very interesting fungal formation and now I can't find him at all.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: He's going to revoke my apprenticeship. He's threatened it eleven times already.

choices.text: [INT 10] The fungal formation sounds important — ask about it.
Point him toward the nearest town.

find.text: His master is three roads over, already writing furiously in a notebook and barely noticed his apprentice was gone. Verin is both relieved and vaguely insulted.

fungus.text: He shows you sketches — a Veil-touched mycorrhizal network mapping the corruption's spread underground. His master has been looking for this for years. You walk him there together.

town.text: "The town! Of course! He always stops at the first inn." He runs. He probably makes it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: We've been walking for nine days. We were told there was a safe camp two days east.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: We've been east for four days. There is no camp.

choices.text: Share your rations and escort them.
[INT 12] Recall the actual location of the safe camp.
Give them gold and directions. You can't take on everyone.

escort.text: A day's travel together. The children fall asleep against your pack. The parents don't speak much, but their eyes do. You reach shelter by nightfall.

recall.text: "It moved. Ash wraith pressure from the north. It's three days southeast now." They straighten with purpose. Direction is everything when you're lost.

gold.text: You press what you can spare into their hands. They look at it for a moment. "Thank you. We'll make it." You hope they're right.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: An absolute absence of sound. Not quiet — void. And in that void, something is trying to communicate using the shape of silence.

choices.text: Be still and receive the message.
Make noise to break the contact.

receive.text: The message fills you without words. A warning: something is closing. A door — not physical. Metaphysical. Closing soon. The silence releases you.

decode.text: Eleven layers of meaning nested in absence. The outermost: warning. The innermost: coordinates. A location in the void that matters enormously.

overwhelm.text: Too much, too fast. You scream without sound. The void releases you, concerned, and you collapse into noise.

break.text: You shout. The silence shatters. Whatever was communicating recoils. You hear it retreat — something vast, moving away.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: Every decision you make here splinters into visible echoes. You can see yourself taking every path at once.

choices.text: Let it run. Let the paths split.
[INT 18] Collapse the wave. Pick one path and commit.
Close your eyes and walk forward.

split.text: For a moment you are everywhere at once. One of your selves finds gold. One of your selves finds a trap. The storm collapses and you are you again, holding both outcomes.

collapse.text: You select. The other selves dissolve. The chosen path opens perfectly. Whatever it leads to, it is exactly right.

lost.text: The storm splits you across probability space. Reassembling yourself takes everything you have.

walk.text: The storm can't disorient you if you don't look at it. Brute pragmatism wins. You emerge on the other side intact and unimpressed.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A hole in the ground that shouldn't exist — it leads somewhere that isn't down. Voices echo from inside. Familiar voices. Your own voice, at different ages.

choices.text: Lower your hand in and listen.
[INT 14] Call into it — ask it a question.
Seal it. Memory wells are Veil feeding traps.

listen.text: You hear yourself from years ago — making a decision you regret. And then you hear yourself tomorrow, calm, certain. "It works out," your future voice says.

ask.text: "What do I need to know?" The well considers. Then: a single name. A place. A time. Clear as daylight.

echo_back.text: Your voice echoes back garbled — all the worst things you've ever said, in a loop. The well laughs in your voice. You cover your ears and walk.

seal.text: You pack earth and stone into it until it's full. The voices fade. The Veil note it as a lost asset. You note it as a victory.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: The ground is the ceiling. The sky is below you. You are standing on thin air above an infinite falling sky, held up by nothing, and the nothing is extremely confident.

choices.text: [DEX 18] Navigate by treating it as a standard physical challenge.
[INT 16] Understand the inversion and exploit it.

trust.text: The nothing holds. Of course it does. At the far end, the zone ends and you step back onto solid ground with everything you started with plus an unshakeable calm.

navigate.text: You treat it like a tightrope in unusual orientation. Your body finds balance where logic says there's none. You cross, exhilarated and intact.

exploit.text: You realize objects fall upward here. You drop your pack, watch it fall to the sky, and retrieve it on the other side with treasures that fell up with it.

panic.text: Your body doesn't agree with the situation. You crawl through on instinct, barely. Your grip on reality is temporarily looser than usual.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A presence — not a figure, not a voice, but a question posed at you by the void itself. You feel it rather than hear it: "Why do you continue?"

choices.text: "Because the people counting on me deserve better than me stopping."
[INT 16] Answer with a question of your own.

no_alternative.text: The void is satisfied by clarity. Not inspired, but satisfied. It gives you something — not wisdom exactly, but a settled certainty that doesn't waver.

others.text: The void considers this for a long time. It is not a concept it fully understands. That you have it anyway seems to matter. Something is given freely.

counter.text: "Why do you ask?" The void is surprised. Then — something like respect. A rare thing from something without feelings. It tells you something it wasn't going to tell you.

no_alternative_fail.text: The void accepts your answer without ceremony. You continue.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A massive wolf is caught in a rusted trap, too exhausted to snarl. Its eyes track you with guarded intelligence.

choices.text: Pry the trap open by force.

befriend.text: The wolf understands. When the trap springs free, it does not run — it falls into step beside you.

free.text: You wrench the trap open. The wolf limps a few steps, turns, and decides you are pack.

flee.text: It panics, tears free, and vanishes into the trees.

leave.text: You walk on. Something howls behind you, long and lonely.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A small owl hatchling chirps pitifully at the base of a towering pine. The nest is impossibly high.

choices.text: [DEX 12] Climb the pine and return it.
Carry it with you — it has no one.

return.text: You reach the nest, tuck the hatchling back, and when you descend the mother lands on your shoulder — gift or judgment, you aren't sure.

fall.text: You slip halfway up and come down hard, bruised and empty-handed.

adopt.text: It stops chirping the moment you tuck it into your cloak. When it grows, it never once flies away for good.

leave.text: You tell yourself the mother will return. You do not look back.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: An egg sits on a cracked altar, streaked with ember-light that pulses weakly like a dying heartbeat.

choices.text: Wrap it in your cloak and carry it.
An egg in a shrine is a trap. Keep moving.

hatch.text: The shell splits. A young drake unfurls wet wings, fixes you with one molten eye, and decides you are nest.

crack.text: Your channeling is uneven. The egg cracks, then goes dark. Something important does not become.

carry.text: By nightfall your bodyheat has done what ritual could not. A small drake coils under your collar, warm as a forge.

leave.text: You walk on. Behind you, the pulse fades.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A sleek black cat watches you from a toppled column. It has not blinked in a long time.

choices.text: Offer it a strip of rations.
[DEX 12] Approach slowly and earn its trust.

feed.text: It accepts the food with ceremonial dignity, then falls in beside you as if the matter were long-decided.

earn.text: You kneel, patient as stone. After a long moment it pads forward and touches its forehead to yours.

flee.text: It flows away like poured ink and is gone.

leave.text: You move on. The eyes follow you for a long time.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A squat crystal golem sits inert in a cavern, its core cracked clean through. A matched gem glitters in the wall above.

choices.text: [INT 14] Pry the gem free and graft it into the core.
Sacrifice 80 gold of gear-glass to patch it.
Walk past. Stones don't ask for help.

repair.text: The gem seats flush. The golem shudders, rises, and regards you with the patience of geology. It follows.

shock.text: A static bolt arcs from the core and flings you back.

patch.text: Your patchwork holds. The golem rises, heavy and loyal, and thuds into step behind you.

leave.text: The cavern stays silent. The golem does not follow.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A pale wisp drifts between the headstones, bumping against one marker, then another, unable to find its own name.

choices.text: Offer a lantern to guide it home.
Dead things should find their own way.

remember.text: It finds the name. It finds you. It chooses you over rest, and circles your shoulder like a patient star.

scatter.text: Your words are wrong. The wisp trembles and scatters like candle smoke.

guide.text: You lead it past the stones to open ground. It circles your lantern once, twice, then nestles beside the flame.

leave.text: It drifts on, nameless.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A skeletal hound crouches in a dead necromancer's workshop, its animating sigil still faintly aglow. It watches you without breath.

choices.text: Speak the old command words aloud.
This is necromancy. Shatter the sigil.

bind.text: The sigil flares, accepts the new master, and the hound rises on quiet bone feet, tail-wagging despite having no tail.

bite.text: Your ritual stumbles. It snaps at you before quieting.

command.text: Its old master's words still work. It heels obediently and pads after you, more loyal than it ever was in life.

destroy.text: You smash the sigil. The hound sighs once, almost grateful, and crumbles.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A tiny crystalline figure is frozen solid in a slab of blue ice, dim light flickering inside it like a held breath.

choices.text: [CON 13] Cradle it against your body until it thaws.
Chip it free carefully with a blade.
Some things freeze for reasons. Leave it.

thaw.text: Hours later it blinks, stretches, and rides your shoulder like it has always belonged there.

frostbite.text: The cold bites through your cloak and into your bones.

chip.text: The last shard of ice falls away and the sprite gasps awake, grateful and bright.

leave.text: You trudge on through the snow.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: An enormous frog, bigger than a shield, is sunk chest-deep in grey mud. Its throat sac pulses a slow, pleading rhythm.

choices.text: Giant frogs are giant. Keep walking.

free.text: With a wet crack of suction the frog pops loose. It burps, considers you gravely, and hops after your boots.

slip.text: The mud takes your footing instead. You come out filthy and empty-handed.

lever.text: Your branch lever holds. The frog rises, shakes off a gallon of muck, and adopts you on the spot.

leave.text: The bubbling stops sometime after you lose sight of it.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A moth the color of deep space batters itself against your lantern. Its wings carry a faint galaxy-shimmer that hurts to look at directly.

choices.text: [INT 14] Speak an arcane word it can understand.
Dim the lantern and hold still.
Swat it away before it unsettles you.

word.text: The word lands. The moth goes still on your glove, blinks an impossible number of eyes, and stays.

burn.text: Your word is almost right. A filament of void-light lashes across your hand.

dim.text: In the dark it settles on your shoulder, silent as a held thought, and does not leave.

swat.text: You miss. It fades through your palm and is gone — but something watches you walk away.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A crimson dragon egg rests in a scorched dragon-nest. Dragon-fire pulses faintly from within — a tiny dragon heart still beats inside the dragon-shell.

choices.text: A dragon-egg is a dragon-curse. Leave it.

hatch.text: The dragon-shell splits. A tiny crimson dragon tumbles out, dragon-wings wet, and imprints on you as dragon-parent.

crack.text: Your dragon-warmth is uneven. The dragon-egg cracks — and goes dark.

cradle.text: By morning the dragon-hatchling has broken free and refuses to leave your shoulder. A dragon-bond is made.

leave.text: You walk on. The dragon-egg pulse fades behind you.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A young frost dragon — a wyrmling — is trapped in a dragon-ice slab. Dragon-frost curls from its nostrils as it watches you with ancient dragon eyes.

choices.text: [STR 14] Shatter the dragon-ice with your weapon.
Melt the dragon-ice slowly with fire.

free.text: The dragon-ice shatters. The frost wyrmling shakes off dragon-frost and decides you are dragon-pack.

slip.text: Your blade glances off the dragon-ice. The wyrmling remains frozen — for now.

melt.text: Hours later the last dragon-ice drips away. The wyrmling stretches and curls against your cloak, dragon-loyal.

leave.text: The frost dragon watches you go, dragon-patient.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A lightning-feathered storm drake — a young dragon — lies grounded on a dragon-cliffside, one dragon-wing torn by wyrm-hunters.

choices.text: [INT 15] Splint the dragon-wing with arcane binding.
Offer the dragon a share of your rations.
A wounded dragon is a dead dragon. Walk away.

heal.text: The dragon-wing knits. The storm drake tests it, crackles with dragon-thunder, and chooses you as dragon-rider.

shock.text: Dragon-lightning arcs through your binding and throws you back.

feed.text: The storm drake accepts the meal with dragon-dignity, then falls in behind you like dragon-shadow.

leave.text: Dragon-thunder mutters as you walk away.

lines.speaker: hero
lines.text: A shadow wyrm — a dragon born of eclipse — coils in a dragon-circle of dead dragon-fire. It has been waiting. For a dragon-slayer, perhaps. Or for you.

pact.text: The shadow wyrm dips its dragon-head. A dragon-pact is forged. It glides into step beside you like a second dragon-shadow.

flee.text: Your dragon-tongue is broken. It hisses and melts into dragon-dark.

kneel.text: The dragon studies you long enough to read your dragon-heart, then decides you are worthy of dragon-company.

fight.text: Dragon-shadow surges. The dragon is gone — and your arm burns where dragon-cold touched it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I have carried this too long. Recite the Third Oath of Mercy and it is yours.

pass.text: She smiles and presses a charm into your palm. It hums with the power to heal a whole party.

fail.text: She shakes her head. "Study more, young one."

leave.text: She nods and returns to her silent vigil.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: The circle of stones pulses. One glyph flickers — unclean energy clings to it.

pass.text: A ring of silver light washes the glyph clean. You feel the rite settle into your hands.

smash.text: The stone cracks; a single feather-shaped charm tumbles out. It is warm to the touch.

fail.text: The glyph snarls and rejects you. You stagger back, unburned but unhelped.

leave.text: You bow respectfully and move on.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Fastest hand on the roads. Prove you can keep up with me.

pass.text: She laughs and pins a wind-charm to your cloak. "When you need extra quickness, just tap."

talk.text: "A thinker! Fine — I was bored anyway." She hands you a totem of raucous flair.

fail.text: She moves, and suddenly she is fifty paces away, waving.

leave.text: She pirouettes and vanishes between the trees.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: A massive warrior in dented plate sits beside a painted totem. They do not speak.

sit.text: After a long hour, they nod once and hand you the painted totem.

arm.text: They grunt approval, then clap a silver ward onto your bracer — it will turn one blow away.

fail.text: They close their eyes. You have been dismissed.

leave.text: The warrior does not so much as blink.

bossDeathDialogs.bossLine: "The Veil... will not... be held back forever..."
bossDeathDialogs.heroLine: "Rest now, Grax. The Breach dies with you."
bossDeathDialogs.narratorLine: A tremor runs through the air. The Veil holds — for now.
Magma hardens around the titan's husk. Steam fills the caldera.

bossDeathDialogs.bossLine: "Hell does not forget. Hell will send another."
bossDeathDialogs.narratorLine: The breach seals with a sound like a door slamming in the void.

bossDeathDialogs.bossLine: "I am the Veil. The Veil... is everywhere..."
bossDeathDialogs.narratorLine: The Veil shudders. A door opens.

bossDeathDialogs.bossLine: "You cannot destroy what was never whole."
bossDeathDialogs.heroLine: "We didn't. We just put it back in the dark."
bossDeathDialogs.narratorLine: Threads of reality knit themselves closed. Something recedes.

bossDeathDialogs.bossLine: "I drew every outcome. In none of them... did I fall."
bossDeathDialogs.narratorLine: Creation breathes. The blueprint crumbles to ash. It is over.

bossDeathDialogs.bossLine: "A thousand years... and this is how... it ends?"
bossDeathDialogs.heroLine: "Every king falls. Even the last one."
bossDeathDialogs.narratorLine: The mountain goes quiet. The dragon's fire fades from the sky.
The prologue ends. The real journey begins.

encounters.name: Warlord's Vanguard
encounters.introText:
A goblin warlord and his honor guard block the road, axes raised.
They have held this crossing for three seasons. No one passes alive.

encounters.name: The Veilspawn Herald
encounters.introText:
Something tears through the fabric between worlds — wrong shape, wrong number of limbs.
It locks eyes with you. It has been waiting.

encounters.name: The Veil Wardens
encounters.introText:
Ancient guardians of the Thornwood — stone-faced, merciless, older than the village they protect.
The shamans chant. The air curdles with old magic.

encounters.name: The Lava Titan
encounters.introText:
The earth splits. A figure of living rock and molten fire heaves itself upright.
The heat alone could kill you. The fists are just a courtesy.

encounters.name: Veil High Priest
encounters.introText:
He stands at the altar, arms spread wide, face ecstatic with devotion.
"You will serve the Veil. One way or another."

encounters.name: Archfiend Malgrath
encounters.introText:
Malgrath fills the throne room with the stench of sulfur and contempt.
"Mortals. Still climbing. Still dying. It does not get old."

encounters.name: The Emberveil Sovereign
encounters.introText:
The Sovereign does not speak. It simply regards you with depthless, burning eyes.
You understand, somehow, that you were always meant to be here.

encounters.name: The Architect
encounters.introText:
It built the abyss you have been walking through. It is still building.
"You have interrupted the design. This is unacceptable."

encounters.name: The Unraveler
encounters.introText:
Reality peels back like old skin to reveal something vast and hungry beneath.
"You were never real," it whispers. "None of this was."

encounters.name: The Ancient Dragon
encounters.introText:
The dragon turns one vast eye on you, each scale the size of a shield.
It has watched empires rise and crumble. You are a very small interruption.

encounters.name: Bahamorth, the Dragon King
encounters.introText:
Bahamorth speaks and the mountain shakes. Every word is an earthquake.
"I am the first flame. The world ends with me. Come, then. Try."

encounters.name: The Vault Guardian
encounters.introText:
The key trembles in your hand as the vault door swings open.
Something ancient that should never have been caged stares back at you.

encounters.name: The Void Scholar
encounters.introText:
He recognises the intelligence in your eyes before you have said a word.
"Good. Someone who might actually understand what I am about to do."

encounters.name: Echo of the Sovereign
encounters.introText:
The Veil remembers those who broke it.
You feel the memory of the Emberveil Sovereign coalesce around you — cold, furious, incomplete.

encounters.name: The First Ember
encounters.introText:
Before the Emberveil there was fire.
Not the fire of dragons or molten stone — the first fire, the one that should have gone out when the world was young.
It has been waiting for someone to find the door.

skillCheck.flavor: A boulder blocks a collapsed passage. Heave it aside to find what lies within.

success.text: You muscle the stone aside. Inside: a cache of coins (+80 gold).

failure.text: The boulder shifts and pins your arm. You wrench free but lose HP.

skillCheck.flavor: Ancient carvings mark a shortcut deeper into the forest. Can you decipher them in the failing light?

success.text: You trace the markings and find a hidden shortcut — and a pouch left behind.

failure.text: You misread the sign and stumble into a thicket. Your vision blurs for 2 rounds in the next fight.

skillCheck.flavor: A glowing tablet bears Veil-cult glyphs. Decipher them to learn their next move.

success.text: You crack the cipher. A Veil supply route is revealed — and a purse the cultists left behind.

failure.text: The tablet discharges when misread, shocking you and alerting a distant patrol. You lose some coin in the scramble.

skillCheck.flavor: A narrow corridor breathes fire with every gust. Endure it to claim the prize beyond.

success.text: Your party grits through the heat. A war-chest sits untouched on the other side.

failure.text: The heat is overwhelming. The party staggers out scorched, losing 15% of their HP.
